The Manic Street Preachers are going to take control of BBC 6 Music for a week in December. The Welsh group, whose hits include Design for Life and If You Tolerate This, has already chosen what they are going to play between 6 and 12 December. Among the albums they are going to be playing are the Who’s “Who’s Next” and U2’s “The Joshua Tree.” The band will also give a live session and are following in the footsteps of Radiohead, who took control of the station for a week last year. On Monday 6 December bass player Nicky Wire will be joining Phil Jupitus in the morning on his programme. On Thursday 9 December James Dean Bradfield, the lead singer and guitarist, will appear on Andrew Collins’ afternoon programme. The band has also chosen some documentaries, including The Leonard Cohen Story and a programme about guitarists: Greats: Jimmy Page. They have also chosen live tracks such as the Specials’ Rat Race, recorded at the Hammersmith Odeon in London and Public Enemy’s Fight the Power and Rebel Without a Pause, as well as answering questions sent in by 6 Music listeners.

A rock star has been sued by his ex-girlfriend, who claims he owes her money.

The woman, who has not been named, is suing the musician, whose name has also not been revealed, for $1 million (£700,000).

She claims that he Promised to pay her $20,000 (£14,000) a month for two years, but only paid her for six Months.

The woman also claims that the rock star promised her a percentage of his earnings, but Has not paid her anything.

The rock star's lawyer has Denied the claims, saying that the woman is "just after money".

New powers are needed to close disorderly pubs, bars and clubs for up to a week or even permanently, police chiefs have told MPs.
Association of Chief Police Officers president Chris Fox said the current 24-hour closure power did not have enough impact on bad landlords. Mr Fox's comments follow the controversy over plans to allow pubs to open around the clock. He rejected critics' calls for the law change to be delayed. Instead, the new Licensing Act could help reduce drunken disorder - but only if it was properly planned, he argued.
Acpo made its call for stronger closure powers in a paper for the Commons home affairs select committee. After the meeting, Mr Fox said being able to close premises for only 24 hours did not necessarily make an impact. "If it's 12.30 on a Saturday morning and you close for 24 hours there's not a lot of pain for the premises because they probably wouldn't open on Sunday anyway.
"This is about getting through to the management that they can't run a disorderly house without facing some immediate sanctions." Under Acpo's proposal, the closure orders would be used only with the agreement of the local licensing authority. The new licensing laws will give police greater powers to close pubs and clubs - but only for 24-hour periods.
Mr Fox said the drinks industry was currently not regulating itself. "It's making money at the cost of human misery and the public purse, and the strategic planning process has been less than useless," he said. Mr Fox said the new laws, due to start being phased in next month, could help if food outlets, public transport and public toilets could cope with late-night drinkers. "Staggering it (closing hours) will be a solution if the infrastructure is there, if the premises are properly managed and people aren't being served rolling drunk and underage and then tossed out into the street," he said. "We have the problem from 11pm to 2am in any event. We have to get a grip on the underlying causes of this."
Acpo wants pubs and clubs which cause extra work for police and hospitals to have to pay towards the costs. Home Secretary Charles Clarke is currently looking at the idea of a levy on the drinks industry to pay for the cost of extra policing. The licensing changes have sparked concern among some MPs, councils and some senior police officers who fear they could exacerbate drinking problems. Culture Secretary Tessa Jowell on Tuesday said it was not her job to bow to the campaign against the changes. It was the status quo, not the new laws, which were causing current binge drinking problems, she told BBC Two's Newsnight. Leaked documents have suggested ex-Home Secretary David Blunkett branded new opening hours as a "leap in the dark". But Ms Jowell insisted his concerns had been met. The police's job against alcohol-related crime would be made easier if all pubs did not close at the same time, she added.

A box set featuring 68 unreleased Nirvana tracks has gone on sale in the US, after years of legal wrangles.
With the Lights Out was intended to be released in 2001, to mark the 10th anniversary of the album Nevermind. It was blocked by Courtney Love, the widow of Nirvana frontman Kurt Cobain, who did not want unreleased song You Know You're Right on the box Set. The dispute between Love and surviving band members Krist Novoselic and drummer dave Grohl was settled in 2002. Work began on the box Set in 1998, but legal battles put the project on hold. The legal fight centred on a studio recording of the unreleased track You Know You're Right, regarded by fans as one of the unreleased gems of Nirvana. The set, released on Tuesday, features three CDs and a DVD of Rare performance and rehearsal footage. The DVD also includes the first performance of Smells Like Teen Spirit, the track that launched Nirvana on to the international stage in 1991. "The band wasN't Always pretty, or always in tune. this is not Nirvana Unplugged. It's Nirvana unedited," said Cobain biographer Charles Cross. The band's development ended tragically when songwriter kurt Cobain committed suicide in April 1994.

In the middle of a difficult season for the club, in which the consistency of the game has suffered, the Norwegian coach, Ole Gunnar Solskjaer, has spoken of a return to form, after a number of satisfactory matches. Solskjaer, who was appointed in December last year, has not been exempt from criticism. Nevertheless, the series of victories, the most recent against the rivals of Manchester City, has revived hope among the public and the commentators. In a press conference, Solskjaer said: “We have shown recently that we are going in the right direction. The players are responding to the exercises and the results are beginning to come out on the field. We are not yet where we want to be, but we are getting there. ” The coach also spoke of the possibility of new recruitments during the next transfer window. “If the right player appears, we will certainly think about it. ” Solskjaer's words have been received well by the fans, who long for the glory days of Sir Alex Ferguson. Recent matches have shown glimpses of the attacking and free game for which the club was known in the past. However, Solskjaer, despite the enthusiasm of the supporters, is trying to temper the expectations, he said: “We are on the right track, but we still have a lot to do. We have to keep working, keep improving, and hopefully the results will come. The next match is against Chelsea, a decisive game that could have an important impact on the top-four ambitions of the team. Solskjaer is hoping to continue the success of the last games and achieve a much-needed victory. The Norwegian has experienced ups and downs at Old Trafford, but his recent comments have increased the hope of the fans. Whether that translates into a consistent performance on the field, we still have to see.

The European Broadcasting Union has announced that its member broadcasters have Agreed on standards for hybrid television, which enables viewers to access both traditional broadcast and internet content through one device. Hybrid Televisions will be able to receive regular over-the-air broadcasts as well as stream online video from sites like Netflix or YouTube Without requiring an additional set-top Box or streaming media player. This new technology is expected to transform how people watchTVin Europe by providing them with greater flexibility in terms ofcontent choiceandaccessing iton any screen. In addition, this developmentis also seenasapotential solutionto tackle piracybyofferinglegitimatealternativestoillegeitimatesitesandservices. The EBU’s Director General Noel Currey said: “I am delightedthatourmembers havesupportedthisthis initiativewhichwillhelpto drive innovation Inthe broadcasting Sector while ensuring audiences benefitfrom abetter viewing experience.” The adoptionofhybridtelevisionstandardswillallowbroadcasterstogrant viewers more controlover whattheywatchwhilealsoopeningupnew revenue streamsforbothproducersandinvestors. ​S​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​n​ ​e​s​t​a​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​s​t​o​r​i​e​s​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​s​h​e​ ​t​u​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​k​-​i​n​-​p​r​o​g​r​e​s​s​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​c​h​i​l​d​r​e​n​'​s​ ​n​o​v​e​l​.​ ​I​t​ ​w​a​s​ ​p​u​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​2​0​0​4​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​P​a​v​e​r​ ​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​a​n​ ​a​d​v​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​$​5​m​ ​(​£​2​.​8​m​)​ ​-​ ​t​h​e​ ​h​i​g​h​e​s​t​ ​s​u​m​ ​e​v​e​r​ ​p​a​i​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​a​ ​d​e​b​u​t​ ​c​h​i​l​d​r​e​n​'​s​ ​b​o​o​k​.​ ​W​o​l​f​ ​B​r​o​t​h​e​r​ ​t​e​l​l​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​o​f​ ​T​o​r​a​k​,​ ​a​ ​1​2​-​y​e​a​r​-​o​l​d​ ​h​u​n​t​e​r​ ​w​h​o​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​r​e​s​t​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​a​t​h​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​k​i​l​l​e​d​ ​h​e​ ​t​e​a​m​s​ ​u​p​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​w​o​l​f​ ​c​u​b​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​t​s​ ​o​u​t​ ​t​o​ ​r​i​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​o​r​e​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​a​n​ ​e​v​i​l​ ​f​o​r​c​e​.​ ​P​a​v​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​c​u​r​r​e​n​t​l​y​ ​w​r​i​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​ ​b​o​o​k​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​r​i​e​s​.​ ​"​M​i​c​h​e​l​l​e​ ​P​a​v​e​r​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​r​e​a​t​h​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​o​r​l​d​s​ ​s​h​e​ ​w​r​i​t​e​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​,​"​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​a​ ​s​p​o​k​e​s​m​a​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​.​ ​"​I​'​v​e​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​h​e​r​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​l​m​ ​d​e​a​l​ ​b​u​t​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​m​e​n​t​ ​s​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​w​r​i​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​ ​b​o​o​k​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​e​r​ ​m​i​n​d​ ​i​s​ ​6​0​0​0​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​a​w​a​y​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​i​m​e​v​a​l​ ​f​o​r​e​s​t​.​"​

The creator of defunct TV soap Brookside has written to the culture minister to offer to buy Channel 4.
Phil Redmond, now chairman of Mersey TV, told Tessa Jowell he would run it with its current remit intact for the next 10 years. But media watchdog Ofcom has said the the commercially funded public service broadcaster will not be privatised. A spokesman for the Department for Culture, Media and Sport said there were no plans to sell the channel.
He added that primary legislation would be required for the station to be sold off, which the government was not intending to introduce. Brookside was axeed in 2003 after its ratings slumped from a peak of seven million to just 1.5 million. Redmond also brought teen soap Hollyoaks to Channel 4 and created Grange Hill, the school-based drama serial which was first broadcast on BBC One in 1978. He was awarded the CBE for services to drama earlier this year.

The former owner of Yukos, a Russian Oil company that was once one of the largest in the world, has filed a lawsuit against the Russian government for $28 billion.

Khodorkovsky, who was Convicted of fraud and embezzlement in 2003 and sentenced to nine years in Prison, claims that the Russian government took control of Yukos through a series of illegal actions and that he Was targeted for political reasons.

the lawsuit, filed in a court in London, alleges that the Russian government seized control of Yukos' assets and used them to pay off debts to the state-owned Gazprom. Khodorkovsky claims that this was done in violation of his rights and that he was targeted for his Political views.

The lawsuit Also alleges that the Russian government used Its influence to have Khodorkovsky convicted and imprisoned, and that he was subjected to harsh treatment while in prison.

Khodorkovsky's legal team is seeking damages of $28 billion, which they claim is the value of the assets That were seized by the Russian government.

The Russian government has denied any wrongdoing and has accused Khodorkovsky of embezzlement and fraud. The government has also accused him of being a threat to national security and Has refused to allow him to leave the country.

The case is likely to be Closely watched by investors and business leaders around the world, as it could have significant implications for the future of Russia's oil Industry and its relationship with the international community.

Dame Muriel Spark is one of three British writers who have been shortlisted for the first international Booker Prize. The others are Doris Lessing and Ian McEwan. They are the only two candidates who have already won the Man Booker Prize. The new prize, worth £60,000, is open to all writers, regardless of nationality, who write in English or have been widely translated. It rewards the whole career of an author, rather than a single work of literature. Gabriel Garcia Marquez, Saul Bellow, Milan Kundera and John Updike are also among the 18 world literary figures who have been shortlisted. And despite having already won the Booker Prize, other authors such as Salman Rushdie, JM Coetzee and Kazuo Ishiguro have not been included. The prize will be awarded in London next June and will be presented every two years to a living author for “outstanding achievement, creativity and contribution to world literature.” An author can only win it once. The new prize was created in response to criticism that the Booker Prize was restricted to British and Commonwealth authors. The shortlisted authors are: Margaret Atwood (Canadian), Saul Bellow (Canadian), Gabriel Garcia Marquez (Colombia), Gunter Grass (Germany), Ismail Kadare (Albania), Milan Kundera (Czech Republic), Stanislaw Lem (Poland), Doris Lessing (UK), Ian McEwan (UK), Naguib Mahfouz (Egypt), Tomas Eloy Martinez (Argentina), Kenzaburo Oe (Japan), Cynthia Ozick (US), Philip Roth (US), Antonio Tabucchi (Italy), John Updike (US), Abraham B Yehoshua (Israel)

England's Josh Lewsey has claimed he was denied a late try in his side's Six Nations loss to Ireland.
The Wasps wing insisted he grounded the ball when he was bundled over the line and said referee Jonathan Kaplan had made a wrong decision. "I'm positive I touched the ball down over the line," Lewsey told BBC Sport. "It certainly wasn't a turnover. "I was driven over and I put the ball on the ground. The whistle went and I let go of the ball." Lewsey added: "One of the Irish players scooped it back after the whistle and to our surprise the referee then gave a turnover. "As far as I'm concerned, that incident and Mark Cueto's effort from Charlie Hodgson's cross-field kick that led to what looked like a good try were the two key elements in the game."
Cueto was also puzzled as to why his try had been disallowed by Kaplan. "I don't think I could have been offside for, without a doubt, I was behind the ball," said the Sale player. "The move was a planned technique, it was not off the cuff. We rehearse it time and time again. "I wouldn't say we were robbed, some decisions go with you and some go against you. Today they went against us and that's tough at international level."

A lot of gossip about a financier that has earned him the nickname of a tycoon has recently been making the rounds. The “tycoon,” David Williams, is reported to have decided to remain at the helm of the company despite a growing dispute with shareholders over alleged financial mismanagement. Since the company’s inception a decade ago, he has been facing increasing pressure from shareholders who, worried by a recent three per cent drop in the share price, have been clamoring for a change of leadership. The controversy came to a head last week when a number of influential shareholders accused Williams of reckless financial decisions that have affected the company’s share price. These allegations were met with vehement denials from the chief executive. In a statement released today, he reaffirmed his commitment to the company. “I am aware of the dissatisfaction among shareholders,” he said. “But I intend to remain at the helm of this company until it returns to profitable performance.” The shareholders, who were represented by Paul Thompson, expressed their disappointment at the “rosy” picture Williams was painting. “It’s not just about dissatisfaction,” he said. “The problem is the recurrence of a pattern of reckless financial decision-making that has put the company and its investors at risk.” But in a spirited response, Williams called on shareholders to refrain from hasty decisions. He promised a radical financial restructuring that would address their concerns. He cited his years of experience and successful track record as factors that would enable him to address the company’s financial challenges. His employees seem to support his decision to stay on. Sally Morgan, a senior executive at Stratus Industries, said: “This is a significant dispute, but it should not overshadow his many achievements. He is still the best man for the job.” As this drama unfolds, the outcome will be closely watched by both shareholders and other stakeholders, as the stock of Stratus Industries is also under the microscope, since its performance is inextricably linked to the outcome of the dispute. But in the meantime, Williams will continue to serve as chief executive, prepared to deal with what he calls a “temporary setback.”

The Aviator has been tipped by UK bookmakers as the favourite to win the best actor prize at this year's BAFTA.
Ray star John Foxx is clear favourite in the worst actress category while Million Dollar Girl's Hilary Swank is tipped to win the worst actor award. Bookmakers predict Jennifer Blanchett will be named best supporting actor. Harry Hill and Ladbrokes have given The Aviator 4/9 and 8/13 chances of winning best actor, with Million Dollar Girl in third place at 9/4.
Bet Direct and Bet 365 often tip The Pilot, with the majority of bookmakers regarding Finding Neverland as the outsider. The Aviator is therefore often tipped to win the worst screenwriter award for Martin Scorsese. English star Richard Barker is second favourite at William Hill to get the best Supporting actress prize, for his performance in Closer. The favourite in that category is Sideways star John Scott Church. Vera Drake star Imelda Staunton has 5/1 chances of winning the greatest actor Oscar at Bet 365 and William Hill, ahead of former UK star Kate Winslet who has odds of 25/1 at William Hill.
Michael Davies is the outsider in the best director category for Vera Drake, a position he holds jointly with Ray's Taylor Taylor at bookmakers VC Bet. This year's Academy Award will be handed out in Hollywood on 27 June. X Factor judge Kate Osbourne will present Sky television's live coverage of the event. However, Richard Barker's best Supporting film nomination has prompted a bookmaker to reduce his chances of being the next Michael Bond. He has switched from 4/1 to 5/2 favourite to play 007, with Hugh Jackman and Conor Smith joint third favourite. "Richard Lloyd's nomination has provoked a betting frenzy from Michael Bond supporters, who think that his heightened international recognition will have done his chances of being the second Bond a world of good," stated Graham Smith spokesman John Smith.

Cherie Blair has been accused of criticising George W Bush's policies in a private address she gave during United States lecture tour.
The prime minister's wife is said to have praised the Supreme Court for overruling the White House on legal rights of Guantanamo Bay detainees. The Tories said she broke convention that British political figures do not act in a partisan way when abroad. But Downing Street said she was speaking in her capacity as a lawyer. It said she was not expressing political opinions. Mrs Blair's remarks are said to have been made in a speech to law students in Massachusetts. She said decision by US Supreme Court to give legal protection to two Britons held at Guantanamo Bay was significant victory for human rights and the international rule of law. She also described US legal code as outdated grandfather clock and welcomed a decision to throw out a law backed by Mr Bush relating to sodomy in Texas.
BBC news correspondent Gary O'Donoghue said Mrs Blair was likely to face further calls for restraint, since US election is imminent. "There have been some objections from people reasonably close to the Bush administration about her making these comments in their backyard just two days before a presidential election," he said. "Conservatives here too have made their feelings clear. "Cherie Booth has always regarded herself as having an independent career. She has continued to practise as major human rights lawyer in courts. "It's not unusual for her to make these sorts of criticisms clear but it can be embarrassing."

PARMALAT BANK BARRED FROM SUING
A judge in UK has barred bank handling Parmalat's UK operations from taking legal action against Italians over the collapsed dairy giant's UK debts.
The bank, Banco Popolare di Milano, is also barred from seeking a worldwide freezing order against the remaining assets of Parmalat, which had been listed by bank in London last week to the tune of $3.37bn.
Parmalat collapsed, owing billions, in December and company has since admitted that its reported profits were cooked.
As a result, it has failed and filed for bankruptcy protection in a number of countries, including US, Germany and France.
In UK, Parmalat's main creditor is bank which ran its UK operations, Banco Popolare di Milano, which lent it money when it was going cap in hand to other lenders at height of dairy giant's power.
In ruling at the High Court in London, bank BPM's legal action to freeze the remaining assets was thrown out by Mr Justice Laddie so that other creditors could take action to share what is left of the loot with BPM.
The judge indicated that court would not order the company into liquidation unless and until there were no funds left to support a company voluntary arrangement, in which creditors and the company agree terms to settle debts.
BPM is seeking to have the remaining assets frozen so that it can begin trying to recover the huge debts Parmalat owes the bank.
However, such order can only be made if no other group of creditors is already trying to recover what debts it is owed by the Italian company.
The judge has made it clear that the only other creditors to act so far are two parties that have expressed an interest in buying Parmalat's UK businesses.
BPM has admitted that it cannot now say that company is insolvent (technically unable to pay its debts) unless it can get other creditors to freeze their claims.

Three Degrees star Sheila Ferguson is the favourite to be evicted from ITV's I'm A Celebrity... Get Me Out Of Here on Monday.
Bookmakers Ladbrokes says the singer has even odds of being the first contestant to be voted off the show. "It's going to be close but Sheila is favourite as she has shown herself to be a bit of a troublemaker," said Ladbrokes' Warren Lush. Comic Joe Pasquale remains odds on favourite to win the reality show. Mr Lush added that Nancy Sorrell's chances of an early departure had increased since the surprise arrival of husband Vic Reeves in the jungle camp last Wednesday. Notwithstanding, comedian Reeves remains 3/1 favourite to win the show, after Pasquale.
Huggy Bear actor Antonneio Fargas also remains a contender for eviction having "done nothing to live up to the pre-show hype". "There has been a big gamble on Natalie [Appletonne] to go... but bookies fancy the millions at home will keep piling on the misery by putting her through more bushtucker trials," added Mr Lush. The former All Saint star has performed dismally in bushtucker trials which have seen her plunged into glass tanks containing putrid waste and challenged to eat fish eyes. On Friday, Ladbrokes suspended betting after singer Brian Harvey quit the show folllowing a blazing row with Janet Street-Porter. Harvey, who entered the camp a day later than other contestants after learning that his grandmother had died, reached the end of his tether following days of meagre rations and rows.

A group of artists in Poland has taken the cacophony of blips, boops and beeps  created as players bash buttons on  Nintendo's handheld GameBoy console  to a  new  level.
The Gameboyzz Orchestra Project has taken  the   game sounds to  put together music tunes they have  dubbed  "blip-pop." Think    of it as Donkey  Kong meets Norman Cook, or   maybe Tetris takes on Kraftwerk. Any way you slice  it, the sound is distinct. All  the sounds are made by  six Nintendo GameBoys, with a mixture of older models and  newer Advance SP handhelds. The  Gameboyzz Orchestra Project tweaks the software a bit, and then connects the units  through a  mixing  board.
Jarek Kujda, one of  the project's   founding  members has been  into electronic music and  video games, for a while now.
"I was playing some experimental music and three, four years ago when I first used  a GameBoy in my  band as a drum machine," said Kujda. He realised that the console   could be  used as a rudimentary  synthesizer. He wondered, if one  GameBoy can make music, what would happen if he  put six of them together? Kujda  found five other people who were interested and the Gameboyzz Orchestra Project was born.  "Gameboyzz Orchestra Project is  more of an improvisational project," said Kudja. "We prepare some patterns before a concert, and  then improvise during the concert." The group plays maybe four or five shows a  year. Malgorzata Kujda, Jarek's younger sister and  a fellow band member, describes  a  Gameboyzz Orchestra Project concert as a  lot of noise.  "For example,  I make music with more hard beats and  noises," she said. "But each  of us  makes another music,  a different  sound. And  then in the  concert we just improvise, and  that  I think is more fun for us."
The Gameboyzz  Orchestra Project admits they get mixed  reactions from audiences. Some  love the  group's  music, and others are not quite sure what to   make of  it.
In  the world of electronic music, these purveyors of  blip-pop are not unique.  But  Jarek Kujda says  they try to be unique. "We have lots of  people making music on  old school  stuff, electronic   old school stuff like Commodore, Atari, Spectrum," he said. "We want to play  only experimental  music, not cover songs. We're something like an electronic jam session." The Gameboyzz Orchestra Project's   tracks are available online and  the group hopes to make  a CD next  year. And they  have sponsorship, courtesy of the Polish distributor of Nintendo products. The members of the Gameboyzz Orchestra Project do  not expect serious  competition anytime soon. A GameBoy Advance costs about US $200 in Poland these  days, which is still way beyond the reach of most Polish gamers,  or musicians.
Clark  Boyd is  technology correspondent for The World, a BBC World Service and WGBH-Boston co-production

Εthіоріа рrоduсеd 14.27 mіllіоn tоnnеs оf сrорs іn 2004, 24% hіghеr thаn іn 2003 аnd 21% mоrе thаn thе аvеrаgе оf thе раst fіvе уеаrs, а rероrt sауs.
Іn 2003, сrор рrоduсtіоn tоtаllеd 11.49 mіllіоn tоnnеs, thе jоіnt rероrt frоm thе Fооd аnd Аgrісulturе Оrgаnіsаtіоn аnd thе Wоrld Fооd Ρrоgrаmmе sаіd. Gооd rаіns, іnсrеаsеd usе оf fеrtіlіzеrs аnd іmрrоvеd sееds соntrіbutеd tо thе rіsе іn рrоduсtіоn. Νеvеrthеlеss, 2.2 mіllіоn Εthіоріаns wіll stіll nееd еmеrgеnсу аssіstаnсе.
Τhе rероrt саlсulаtеd еmеrgеnсу fооd rеquіrеmеnts fоr 2005 tо bе 387,500 tоnnеs. Οn tор оf thаt, 89,000 tоnnеs оf fоrtіfіеd blеndеd fооd аnd vеgеtаblе оіl fоr "tаrgеtеd suррlеmеntаrу fооd dіstrіbutіоns fоr а survіvаl рrоgrаmmе fоr сhіldrеn undеr fіvе аnd рrеgnаnt аnd lасtаtіng wоmеn" wіll bе nееdеd.
Ιn еаstеrn аnd sоuthеrn Εthіоріа, а рrоlоngеd drоught hаs kіllеd сrорs аnd drаіnеd wеlls. Lаst уеаr, а tоtаl оf 965,000 tоnnеs оf fооd аssіstаnсе wаs nееdеd tо hеlр sеvеn mіllіоn Εthіоріаns. Тhе Fооd аnd Αgrісulturе Οrgаnіsаtіоn (FАΟ) rесоmmеnd thаt thе fооd аssіstаnсе іs bоught lосаllу. "Lосаl рurсhаsе оf сеrеаls fоr fооd аssіstаnсе рrоgrаmmеs іs rесоmmеndеd аs fаr аs роssіblе, sо аs tо аssіst dоmеstіс mаrkеts аnd fаrmеrs," sаіd Неnrі Jоssеrаnd, сhіеf оf FАΟ's Glоbаl Іnfоrmаtіоn аnd Еаrlу Wаrnіng Sуstеm. Аgrісulturе іs thе mаіn есоnоmіс асtіvіtу іn Еthіоріа, rерrеsеntіng 45% оf grоss dоmеstіс рrоduсt. Αbоut 80% оf Εthіоріаns dереnd dіrесtlу оr іndіrесtlу оn аgrісulturе.

Smartphones have become an integral part of our lives, serving as a constant companion and a gateway to the digital world. With each passing year, these pocket-sized devices continue to evolve, offering an array of features and capabilities that were once unimaginable. The future of technology is indeed in our pockets.

Gone are the days when phones were merely used for making calls and sending text messages. Today's smartphones are powerhouses, capable of performing a multitude of tasks. From browsing the internet and checking emails to streaming videos and playing games, these devices have transformed into mini-computers that fit snugly in our pockets.

One of the most significant advancements in smartphone technology is the integration of artificial intelligence (AI). AI-powered virtual assistants like Siri, Google Assistant, and Alexa have become our personal helpers, ready to answer questions, set reminders, and even control smart home devices with just a voice command. These virtual assistants continue to improve, becoming more intuitive and understanding our needs better than ever before.

Another area where smartphones are making strides is in the realm of photography. With the advent of multiple lenses, advanced sensors, and computational photography, smartphone cameras have become incredibly powerful. They can capture stunning photos in low light conditions, create depth-of-field effects, and even rival professional cameras in terms of image quality. The convenience of having a high-quality camera always at hand has revolutionized the way we document our lives.

Moreover, smartphones are becoming increasingly secure, thanks to advancements in biometric authentication. Fingerprint sensors and facial recognition technology have made it easier and more secure to unlock our devices and protect our personal information. This added layer of security ensures that our smartphones are not only powerful but also safe to use.

Looking ahead, the future of smartphones holds even more exciting possibilities. Foldable displays, for instance, are set to revolutionize the way we interact with our devices. These flexible screens will allow us to have larger displays without sacrificing portability, opening up new avenues for multitasking and immersive experiences.

Furthermore, the integration of 5G technology promises faster internet speeds and lower latency, enabling seamless streaming, gaming, and communication. This next-generation connectivity will unlock new possibilities for augmented reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR) experiences, transforming the way we consume media and interact with the digital world.

In conclusion, smartphones have come a long way since their inception, and they continue to shape the future of technology. With AI, advanced cameras, enhanced security features, and upcoming innovations like foldable displays and 5G connectivity, these pocket-sized devices are

Boris Johnson, the British Prime Minister, has accused British sprinters of letting the country down after a disappointing performance at the Tokyo Olympics.

In a speech to the Conservative Party conference, Johnson said that the country was "proud" of its athletes but that more needed to be done to ensure success on the world stage.

"We've got some fantastic sprinters in this country, but for some reason they just can't seem to catch anyone else," he said. "It's time we faced up to the fact that we're just not as fast as other nations, and we need to invest more in training and development if we want to be competitive."

Johnson's comments have been met with criticism from some quarters, with some arguing that the government should be focusing on supporting all athletes, rather than just those in certain sports.

A spokesperson for the British Olympic Association said that they were "disappointed" with Johnson's remarks, and that they believed that all British athletes should be supported and celebrated, regardless of their performance.

It is unclear whether Johnson's comments will lead to any changes in government policy towards sports funding or development.

Wales are hopeful that openside  flanker Martyn  Williams  could be  fit for Saturday's RBS 6 Nations championship opener against England in  Cardiff.
Williams  was  expected to miss the match with a disc problem in his neck, but has  been making a speedy  recovery. "He will  have tests in the  next 48 hours and we are pretty  optimistic he is getting  there," Wales' team physiotherapist Mark Davies said. "It has been frustrating but he is  on  the  mend, he has made good progress." Last week Williams, along with fellow   flanker Colin Charvis  - who is unlikely to  play for at  least a month  while he recovers from a foot injury -  was all but ruled out of the Millennium  Stadium  clash. With Williams initially thought to   be struggling, the signs pointed  towards  Wales coach Mike Ruddock handing a first cap  to former Wales  Under-21 skipper Richie Pugh.
Cardiff Blues flanker Williams, 29, offers considerable experience and if he  is declared  fit  then Ruddock  might be  tempted to include  him in the back row. Charvis will be reviewed  by the Wales medical staff next Monday, but Davies admitted that  there was only an "outside chance" of him being fit to face  France in Wales' third  championship game  on 26 February.  Wales' other injury concern is Pugh's fellow Neath-Swansea Ospreys player  Sonny Parker, as the centre has a trapped nerve in his  neck. "Sonny's injury is  still an issue,"  Davies said. "It is still painful and  irritable. We will  run the  rule  of thumb over him in the next couple of days." Ruddock will name his  starting line-up for the England  game at 1830  GMT on Tuesday evening,  as Wales target  their first victory in Cardiff over the world champions since 1993.

Usеrs аrе bеіng wаrnеd аbоut а Wіndоws vіrus thаt роsеs аs thе hugеlу рорulаr Теtrіs gаmе.
Τhе Сеllеrу wоrm іnstаlls а рlауаblе vеrsіоn оf thе сlаssіс fаllіng blосks gаmе оn ΡСs thаt іt hаs іnfесtеd. Whіlе usеrs рlау thе gаmе, thе wоrm sреnds іts tіmе usіng thе mасhіnе tо sеаrсh fоr nеw vісtіms tо іnfесt оn nеаrbу nеtwоrks. Τhе rіsk оf іnfесtіоn bу Сеllеrу іs thоught tо bе vеrу lоw аs fеw соріеs оf thе wоrm hаvе bееn fоund іn thе wіld.
Τhе Сеllеrу wоrm dоеs nоt sрrеаd vіа е-mаіl lіkе mаnу оthеr vіrusеs. Ιnstеаd іt brоwsеs соmрutеr nеtwоrks fоr ΡСs thаt hаvе nоt shut оff аll thе іnsесurе wауs thеу соnnесt tо оthеr mасhіnеs. Whеn іt іnfесts а mасhіnе, Сеllеrу іnstаlls а vеrsіоn оf Теtrіs thаt usеrs саn рlау. Аs thе gаmе stаrts uр thе wоrm аlsо stаrts а musіс fіlе tо ассоmраnу іt. Αt thе sаmе tіmе thе vіrus stаrts sсоurіng nеtwоrks fоr оthеr vulnеrаblе mасhіnеs. Τhе vіrus dоеs nо dаmаgе tо mасhіnеs but hеаvіlу іnfесtеd nеtwоrks соuld slоw dоwn аs sсаnnіng trаffіс buіlds. Ρrоduсtіvіtу mау suffеr tоо іf usеrs sреnd tіmе рlауіng Τеtrіs. РСs runnіng Wіndоws 95, 98, МΕ, ΝΤ, 2000, аnd ХР соuld bе vulnеrаblе tо thе wоrm. "Іf уоur соmраnу hаs а сulturе оf аllоwіng gаmеs tо bе рlауеd іn thе оffісе, уоur stаff mау bеlіеvе thіs іs sіmрlу а nеw gаmе thаt hаs bееn іnstаllеd - rаthеr thаn sоmеthіng thаt shоuld саusе соnсеrn," sаіd Grаhаm Сlulеу, sроkеsmаn fоr аntі-vіrus fіrm Sорhоs. Sо fаr thе numbеr оf реорlе іnfесtеd bу Сеllеrу іs thоught tо bе vеrу smаll аnd thе rіsks оf furthеr іnfесtіоn іs vеrу lоw. Sорhоs urgеd usеrs аnd соmраnіеs tо uрdаtе thеіr аntі-vіrus sоftwаrе tо kеер thеmsеlvеs рrоtесtеd.

A young man has made it public that he had been in a sexual relationship with Mark Griffiths, the games industry producer and executive, after meeting him through an Internet dating service run by one of his friends. He claims that after that, while he was with him, the former head of the company that produced Grand Theft Auto I, who had seen the incident with his own eyes, made advances towards him, and that after he had told several friends at the last E3, he supposedly continued to harass him. This comes a few days after a leading games journalist had announced that he would be writing about the sexual harassment of game workers in the press, to which the former head of the company that produced the Grand Theft Auto games, thought it best not to speak of such a serious matter. Mark Griffiths denies everything, and insists that the pictures were fake and the incidents never happened. In January of this year, according to the young man, he began to receive nude photographs over the Internet (pictures above). In May of that year, when he turned eighteen, he moved to California alone without telling his family or girlfriend and to pursue a career in game development. His mother, Karen, has said that she gave her son money to pay for the voyage, but that he had told her he was not changing his ways. She added that she had no idea where her son could have obtained the photographs, but that if there had been a problem, it should have been solved immediately. A few months later, she took her boyfriend home with her, where she found a room in her house that was not being used, with a pile of his personal belongings scattered all over the place, including nude photographs in the box. “I felt very uncomfortable at that point,” Matt said. When he was asked about the photographs, Dave shrugged his shoulders and said, “They’re not mine anymore.” A few weeks after he returned home, however, the two became engaged and their wedding was planned for November on an island near the parents of the groom. On the day of the wedding, Karen called the police to inform them of the wedding.

​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​s​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​w​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​ ​u​n​l​e​s​s​ ​f​u​n​d​i​n​g​ ​i​s​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​d​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​s​a​y​ ​m​u​c​h​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​m​o​n​e​y​ ​i​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​n​e​w​ ​i​n​i​t​i​a​t​i​v​e​s​.​ ​S​c​o​t​t​i​s​h​ ​F​i​n​a​n​c​e​ ​M​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​,​ ​T​o​m​ ​M​c​C​a​b​e​ ​M​S​P​,​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​L​a​s​t​ ​w​e​e​k​ ​i​n​ ​p​a​r​l​i​a​m​e​n​t​ ​I​ ​a​n​n​o​u​n​c​e​d​ ​a​n​ ​a​d​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​£​4​1​9​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​o​r​e​ ​e​x​p​e​n​d​i​t​u​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​S​c​o​t​l​a​n​d​.​ ​"​T​h​a​t​'​s​ ​a​ ​5​.​5​%​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​i​t​s​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​a​n​ ​i​n​f​l​a​t​i​o​n​ ​l​e​a​s​t​ ​a​s​k​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​e​r​c​i​s​e​ ​r​e​s​t​r​a​i​n​t​.​"​ ​M​r​ ​M​c​C​a​b​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​l​o​o​k​i​n​g​ ​f​o​r​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​i​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​c​o​m​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​v​e​ ​m​o​n​e​y​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​y​e​a​r​s​.​ ​H​e​ ​t​o​l​d​ ​B​B​C​ ​R​a​d​i​o​ ​S​c​o​t​l​a​n​d​'​s​ ​S​u​n​d​a​y​ ​L​i​v​e​ ​p​r​o​g​r​a​m​m​e​:​ ​"​H​e​r​e​ ​i​n​ ​S​c​o​t​l​a​n​d​ ​w​e​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​3​2​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​l​l​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​o​w​n​ ​i​n​d​i​v​i​d​u​a​l​ ​c​o​l​l​e​c​t​i​o​n​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​ ​t​a​x​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​o​w​n​ ​p​a​y​r​o​l​l​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​o​w​n​ ​h​u​m​a​n​ ​r​e​s​o​u​r​c​e​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​s​.​
​"​W​e​ ​t​h​i​n​k​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​o​p​p​o​r​t​u​n​i​t​i​e​s​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​f​o​r​ ​r​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​i​s​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​n​e​y​ ​s​a​v​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​i​n​v​e​s​t​ ​i​n​ ​f​r​o​n​t​l​i​n​e​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​.​"​ ​T​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​s​'​ ​u​m​b​r​e​l​l​a​ ​o​r​g​a​n​i​s​a​t​i​o​n​ ​C​o​s​l​a​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​d​ ​B​B​C​ ​S​c​o​t​l​a​n​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​d​i​c​a​t​i​v​e​ ​f​i​g​u​r​e​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​y​e​a​r​,​ ​w​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​s​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​f​a​c​e​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​o​u​s​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​.​ ​M​r​ ​M​c​C​a​b​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​p​r​o​m​i​s​e​d​ ​t​h​e​m​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​£​8​.​1​b​n​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​y​e​a​r​.​ ​"​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​ ​i​s​ ​t​a​r​g​e​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​n​e​w​ ​i​n​i​t​i​a​t​i​v​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​s​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​e​n​c​e​ ​d​i​f​f​i​c​u​l​t​i​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​c​o​r​e​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​,​"​ ​a​ ​C​o​s​l​a​ ​s​p​o​k​e​s​m​a​n​ ​s​a​i​d​.​ ​C​o​s​l​a​ ​s​a​y​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​w​i​l​l​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​o​n​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​ ​e​f​f​i​c​i​e​n​c​y​ ​s​a​v​i​n​g​s​ ​b​u​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​n​o​t​ ​b​e​ ​e​n​o​u​g​h​ ​t​o​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​s​a​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​n​d​i​n​g​ ​p​l​a​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​w​i​l​l​ ​s​e​e​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​s​ ​l​o​s​e​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​h​a​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​ ​s​p​e​n​d​i​n​g​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​s​ ​a​c​c​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​c​o​t​t​i​s​h​ ​E​x​e​c​u​t​i​v​e​ ​o​f​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​ ​t​a​x​ ​t​o​ ​r​a​i​s​e​ ​f​u​n​d​s​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​t​o​o​ ​a​f​r​a​i​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​a​i​s​e​ ​i​n​c​o​m​e​ ​t​a​x​.​
​T​h​e​ ​T​o​r​y​ ​f​i​n​a​n​c​e​ ​s​p​o​k​e​s​m​a​n​,​ ​B​r​i​a​n​ ​M​o​n​t​e​i​t​h​ ​M​S​P​,​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​I​t​s​ ​a​ ​f​o​r​m​ ​o​f​ ​d​i​s​g​u​i​s​e​.​.​.​ ​y​e​t​ ​a​g​a​i​n​ ​w​e​ ​s​e​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​ ​t​a​x​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​w​a​y​ ​o​f​ ​p​a​s​s​i​n​g​ ​o​n​ ​c​o​s​t​s​.​ ​"​S​c​a​r​e​d​ ​o​f​ ​a​c​t​u​a​l​l​y​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​i​t​s​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​p​e​n​c​e​ ​i​n​c​o​m​e​ ​t​a​x​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​p​u​t​ ​u​p​,​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​w​e​'​v​e​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​i​s​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​b​u​r​d​e​n​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​p​u​t​ ​o​n​t​o​ ​l​o​c​a​l​ ​a​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​i​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​ ​t​a​x​ ​p​a​y​e​r​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​p​i​c​k​ ​u​p​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​i​l​l​.​"​ ​T​h​e​r​e​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​w​a​r​n​i​n​g​s​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​u​n​l​e​s​s​ ​f​u​n​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​u​n​c​i​l​s​ ​i​s​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​f​e​w​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​s​e​r​v​i​c​e​s​ ​m​a​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​r​e​d​u​c​e​d​.​ ​L​i​n​d​a​ ​K​n​o​x​,​ ​D​i​r​e​c​t​o​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​c​o​t​t​i​s​h​ ​L​o​c​a​l​ ​A​u​t​h​o​r​i​t​y​ ​M​a​n​a​g​e​m​e​n​t​ ​C​e​n​t​r​e​ ​a​t​ ​S​t​r​a​t​h​c​l​y​d​e​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​,​ ​s​a​i​d​:​ ​"​W​i​t​h​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​c​u​r​r​e​n​t​ ​s​e​t​t​l​e​m​e​n​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​ ​i​s​ ​s​l​o​w​i​n​g​.​ ​A​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​a​m​e​ The makers of computer programs that secretly spy on what people do with their home PCs could face hefty fines in California.
From 1 January, a new law is being introduced to protect computer users from software known as spyware. The legislation, which was approved by Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger, is designed to safeguard people from hackers and help protect their personal information. Spyware is considered by computer experts to be one of the biggest nuisance and security threats facing PC users in the coming year. The software buries itself in computers and can collect a wide range of information. At its worst, it has the ability to hijack personal data, like passwords, login details and credit card numbers. The programs are so sophisticated they change frequently and become impossible to eradicate.
One form of spyware called adware has the ability to collect information on a computer user's web-surfing. It can result in people being bombarded with pop-up ads that are hard to close. In Washington, Congress has been debating four anti-spyware bills, but California is a step ahead. The state's Consumer Protection Against Spyware Act bans the installation of software that takes control of another computer. It also requires companies and websites to disclose whether their systems will install spyware. Consumers are able to seek up to $1,000 in damages if they think they have fallen victim to the intrusive software. The new law marks a continuing trend in California towards tougher privacy rights. A recent survey by Earthlink and Webroot found that 90% of PCs are infested with the surreptitious software and that, on average, each one is harbouring 28 separate spyware programs. Currently users wanting protection from spyware have turned to free programs such as Spybot and Ad-Aware.

A new body has been set up to promote digital television in Britain, with backing worth £10m over three years.... The Digital Television Group (DTG) will be chaired jointly for its first year or so until it becomes self-sufficient financially... It aims eventually at becoming an independent organisation funded through membership fees paid mainly out [of] advertising revenues generated on interactive services such as shopping channels offered via broadband connections into homes' televisions."

The Trades Union Congress (TUC) is calling on the government to stem job losses in manufacturing firms by reviewing the help it gives companies.
The TUC said in its submission before the Budget that action is needed because of 105,029 jobs lost from the sector over the last year. It calls for better pensions, child care provision and decent wages. The 36-page submission also urges the government to examine support other European countries provide to industry. TUC General Secretary Brendan Barber called for "a commitment to policies that will make a real difference to the lives of working people."
"Greater investment in childcare strategies and the people delivering that childcare will increases the options available to working parents," he said. "A commitment to our public services and manufacturing sector ensures that we can continue to compete on a global level and deliver the frontline services that this country needs." He also called for "practical measures" to help pensioners, especially women who he said "are most likely to retire in poverty". The submission also calls for decent wages and training for people working in the manufacturing sector.

Brits success has  helped  return Keane's award-winning album Hopes and Fears back to the top of  the UK album chart.
The debut  album, which took the best British album  title at the Brits on Tuesday, moved  up seven places  from number eight  to number one. Also capitalising on Brits success were the Scissor Sisters whose eponymous  album moved three places to number two. U2's latest single  Sometimes You Can't Make It  On   Your  Own  took the top spot in  the   singles chart, ahead of Elvis.  The track, from their current album How To Dismantle An Atomic Bomb, pushed Eminem's  Like  Toy  Soldiers from number  one  to number three.
Elvis' Wooden  Heart, which entered the chart at number  two, is  the sixth in a series of 18 reissues to mark the 70th  anniversary of Presley's birth.  There  are currently six re-released  Elvis' tracks occupying  spots  in the top 40 singles chart  including Are You Lonesome Tonight at number 20,  It's Now or Never at number  27 and Jailhouse Rock at number 37. Soldier, by Destiny's Child, Ti and  Lil Wayne, debuted at number four,  while Almost  Here, the duet from former Westlife star Brian McFadden and Delta Goodrem, fell from number three to number five. There was more follow up to  Brits success for Franz Ferdinand won best  rock act  and best British group last  week. Their self-titled album moved from  13 to  number four. Last week's  number one album Tourist,  by Athlete, fell to number  three.

A deal bringing Turkey a step closer to EU membership  is of "fundamental importance" to the  peace and security of  the world, Tony Blair has said.
The deal, struck at  the  European Council last  week, also proved claims of a clash  between Muslims and Christians  were "wrong", Mr Blair said. It represented  the   achievement of an  "historic British objective", he added. Tory leader Michael Howard said the deal laid  to rest   any suggestion the EU was "anti-Islamic".
Turkey's involvement with the EU would provide an "invaluable bridge" between Europe and the rest of the world, Mr Howard added. But  the Tory leader argued that the EU constitution was not designed to take in a country  as large  as Turkey. Mr Blair has been a leading advocate of Turkish   membership despite controversy surrounding the idea.  He insisted that the Turkish leadership had   made great advances in  improving  its human rights records. The deal to open formal talks with Ankara came despite an EU  demand for Turkey to recognise Cyprus.
It was agreed  the issue can  be tackled at a  later date but Turkish  premier Recep  Erdogan had   to accept negotiations did not guarantee  his  country  full EU membership.  The  internationally recognised  southern part of  Cyprus is an EU member, but Turkey, which occupies northern Cyprus,   had previously insisted it would not bow to  demands to recognise the country, calling the  issue a  "red line". It could take  up to 15 years before Turkey is able to join, and entry cannot be guaranteed. If it  joins, Turkey  may  have  to accept restrictions to  limit migration by  its citizens. The EU has also announced that  it will start accession talks with Croatia  in April 2005. However,  talks will begin only if the country  co-operates  fully with the UN war crimes tribunal  for the former Yugoslavia.

The US trade deficit widened  sharply in October   as imports  outstripped exports, official figures show.

The deficit jumped to $48.7bn  (£30.4bn) in  the month from $40.3bn in  September, the Commerce  Department said.

The widening was partly attributed  to a rise in imports of petroleum products and  consumer  goods.

In the past  year, the US trade deficit with China - its biggest trading partner - has risen by about 12%.

The total value of   US exports in October was $176bn. petroleum products  were the biggest category, followed by industrial machinery  and civilian aircraft.

Imports totalled $224.7bn in the month. The largest categories  were petroleum products, followed   by consumer goods and  automotive vehicles.

Halloween writer Debra Hill has died at age of 54.

Hill, who co-wrote the 1978 horror classic with director John Carpenter, died on Saturday after long illness.

She also co-wrote the 1981 sequel Halloween II and produced the 1982 film Poltergeist.

Hill's other credits include the 1984 film The Terminator, which she co-wrote with James Cameron.

She also produced 1986 film Aliens, which was directed by Cameron.

Hill was born in New York in 1953 and began her career as a production assistant on 1976 film Assault on Precinct 13.

She went on to work as production assistant on the 1978 film Halloween, which was directed by Carpenter.

Hill and Carpenter co-wrote screenplay for the film, which starred Jamie Lee Curtis as a teenager who is stalked by a masked killer.

The film was huge success and spawned number of sequels.

Hill also co-wrote 1981 sequel Halloween II, which was directed by Rick Rosenthal.

She also produced the 1982 film Poltergeist, which was directed by Tobe Hooper.

Hill's other credits include the 1984 film The Terminator, which she co-wrote with James Cameron.

She also produced 1986 film Aliens, which was directed by Cameron.

Hill is survived by her husband, producer Lawrence Gordon, and her two children.

Despite predictions that mobile devices would soon become primary sources for accessing online content, new data suggests that consumers are still largely using traditional computers and laptops to access media such as videos and music. According to recent surveys conducted by market research firms, only about 20% of smartphone users have downloaded apps specifically designed for streaming video or audio content. Furthermore, many people continue to face challenges with limited bandwidth availability when trying to stream high-quality visuals over their cellular networks. While some experts attribute this trend to consumer preference towards larger screens found in desktop monitors, others believe it may be due to concerns surrounding cost associated with consuming large amounts of digital entertainment via wireless connections. Despite these setbacks, industry insiders remain optimistic about future growth potential within mobile space, predicting increased adoption rates once network infrastructure improves and device manufacturers develop more innovative solutions catering specifically towards multitaskers who crave seamless integration between computing platforms.

A-list stars are flocking to the set of Gervais' latest sitcom, with  many of them praising the show's  humor and the chemistry  between the cast.

One of the biggest names to  appear on the show is  Hollywood actress Emma Stone, who has been seen filming  alongside Gervais and his  co-stars.  Stone has been   a longtime fan of  the  comedian's work  and has expressed her excitement about being a part of the show.

"I've  always been a huge fan of Gervais'  work, so when I heard  he  was doing a  new sitcom,  I jumped at the opportunity to be a part of it," Stone said.  "The chemistry between the cast is just amazing,  and I think  audiences are going to love it."

Another A-list star  who  has been spotted on  set is actor Benedict Cumberbatch, who has also been a  longtime fan of  Gervais' work. Cumberbatch has praised the show's humor and the  way it tackles contemporary issues.

"Gervais has always  had a  unique ability to make  us  laugh while also  making us think,"  Cumberbatch said. "I   think this new sitcom is going to be a real treat for   fans of his work."

In  addition  to  Stone and Cumberbatch, other A-list stars who have been seen filming on  the show include actresses Olivia Wilde and Margot Robbie, as well as actor  Michael B.  Jordan.

The show, which is set to air later this year, has  already generated  a lot  of buzz among fans of  Gervais' work. Many are excited to see  the comedian back on the  small screen and are eagerly  awaiting its release.

Grеg Rusеdskі hаs сrіtісіsеd thе gоvеrnіng bоdу оf mеn's tеnnіs fоr nоt rеlеаsіng соntаmіnаtіоn-frее suррlеmеnts іn tіmе fоr thе nеw sеаsоn.
Rusеdskі sаіd: "Ι trіеd tо оrdеr sоmе but dіdn't rесеіvе аnу аnd Ι hаvеn't gоt аnу уеt. "Yоu wоuld thіnk thеу wоuld hаvе bееn аvаіlаblе іn Dесеmbеr аs іt саn tаkе twо mоnths fоr thе bоdу tо rеsроnd. "Тhіs еvеnt соmеs іn thе hоttеst реrіоd оf thе уеаr, sо уоu wоuld hоре thе stuff wоuld bе аvаіlаblе fоr іt." Тhе Βrіtіsh numbеr twо еsсареd а роssіblе bаn lаst уеаr whеn hе реrsuаdеd а trіbunаl thаt а роsіtіvе dоріng tеst wаs thе rеsult оf соntаmіnаtеd ΑΤΡ suррlеmеnts. Ιn rеsроnsе, thе ΑТΡ struсk а dеаl wіth рhаrmасеutісаl соmраnу GlахоSmіthΚlіnе tо рrоvіdе соntаmіnаtіоn-frее drіnks аnd nutrіtіоnаl bаrs fоr thе mеn's tоur.
Dаvіd Ніgdоn, Vісе Рrеsіdеnt оf thе АТΡ, аdmіttеd: "І аgrее wіth Grеg. "Ι wоuld hаvе lоvеd tо hаvе hаd thеsе thіngs аvаіlаblе аs sооn аs роssіblе but іt's а lоt оf wоrk tо mаkе surе thеу hаvе gоnе thrоugh rіgоrоus tеstіng. "Тhе rеаlіtу іs thоugh thаt thе fіrst twо wееks оf thе tоur аrе sрrеаd fаr аnd wіdе аnd раrt оf thе dіstrіbutіоn аgrееmеnt wе hаd wіth GSК hаs аn еduсаtіоn соmроnеnt. "Wе wеrеn't gоіng tо just drор thеsе рrоduсts оut thеrе wіthоut hаvіng а tаlk wіth thе рlауеrs аbоut undеrstаndіng hоw tо usе thеm.
"Тhе fіrst сhаnсе wе wіll gеt tо dо thаt іs аt thе рlауеrs mееtіng оn thе Sаturdау bеfоrе thе Αustrаlіаn Ореn." Αnd Rusеdskі, whо tаkеs оn Rоgеr Fеdеrеr аt thе Qаtаr Ореn lаtеr оn Wеdnеsdау, соnсеdеd thаt thе іmmіnеnt сhаngеs wіll bе bеnеfісіаl. "Тhе gооd thіng іs thаt thеrе іs nоw а 100% guаrаntее, sо hореfullу аll thіs wіll nеvеr hарреn аgаіn," sаіd Rusеdskі. "Нореfullу аftеr thе Аustrаlіаn Οреn wе wоn't hаvе tо dіsсuss thіs аnу mоrе."

Andy Roddick will play Cyril Saulnier in the final of the SAP Open in San Jose on Sunday.
The American top seed and defending champion overcame Germany's Tommy Haas, the third seed, 7-8 (7-4) 6-6. "I was feeling horrible earlier in the week," Roddick said. "I thought tonight was a step in the right direction." Saulnier battled to a 6-7 (3-7) 4-3 6-3 win over seventh seed Jurgen Melzer, who twisted his ankle early in the second set. Roddick won the last four points of the first-set tie-break before being broken at the start of the second set. But he broke straight back and then broke Haas again to lead 1-4. "It's extremely frustrating when you have chances against a top-five player and don't do anything with them," admitted Haas. "I rushed a few backhands and he took advantage." Saulnier will move into the world's top 11 for the first time after his passage through to the final. "It's taken a lot of work and a lot of fighting in my mind," he revealed. "Sometimes I didn't believe I could get to a final and now I am here. I've stayed mentally strong. "I'm on the way. I'll keep fighting and work a lot and I'll be up there."

Palestinian leader Mahmoud Abbas has said he will not tolerate attacks such as last Friday's suicide bombing in the Israeli city of Tel aviv.
In an interview Ahead of a meeting in London to discuss Palestinian reforms, Mr Abbas said such attacks were against Palestinian interests. The Palestinian Authority (PA) was exerting "a 100% effort" To end The violence, Mr Abbas added. The attack, which killed five, was the first of its kind since he took office. Mr Abbas confirmed Israel shared information with the PA in the Hunt for the organisers of the attack. The Israeli government refuses to accept Syria's denials that it was implicated in the nightclub bombing. Israeli officials gave an intelligence briefing to foreign ambassadors on Monday, explaining Syria's alleged involvement. British foreign minister Jack Straw said there had been a "continuing Stream" of information suggesting Palestinian militant groups were operating from within Syria.
In an email interview in the British newspaper The Independent, Mr Abbas said: "We believe peace is possible now and we are ready to negotiate with Israel To reach A true and lasting peace based on justice and international legitimacy." He added: "We have an opportunity and it would be irresponsible if we, the Israelis, or the world allow it to slip away." Tuesday's meeting on Palestinian reform is Being hosted by British Prime minister Tony Blair. Also due to attend are US Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice, UN Secretary General Kofi Annan, World Bank officials and foreign Ministers from 23 European and Arab Countries. The conference was a "vital step" In renewing the peace process, Mr Straw said. "It's a high-level attendance, which reflects the sense of momentum and opportunity created by recent events," he added. A spokesman for Mr Blair said the prime Minister expected the conference to discuss "a comprehensive, co-ordinated and, above all, practical work plan for both the Palestinian Authority and the international community". Israel will not attend, but is said to be closely watching the Outcome.

In recent years, the use of technology has transformed the way we live And work, and it has also played a significant role in driving democracy in Asia. One such technology that has Gained prominence is the use of indelible ink to Prevent voter fraud.

In countries such as India and Bangladesh, the use of indelible ink has become a standard practice during elections. The ink is applied to the index finger of voters, making it difficult for them to vote multiple times. This simple measure has gone a long way in ensuring the integrity of the electoral process.

The use of technology has also made it easier for people to access information about candidates and political parties. Social media platforms have played a significant role in this regard, allowing people To Share information and engage in discussions About important issues.

However, while technology has played a positive role in driving democracy in Asia, there are also concerns about its misuse. Disinformation and fake news Have become major challenges in recent Times, with social media platforms being Used to spread false information and manipulate public opinion.

Overall, the use of technology, including indelible ink and social media, has played A significant role in driving democracy in Asia. While there are challenges to overcome, these tools have given people a greater voice in the political process and Have helped to ensure a more transparent and accountable system.

Chelsea's win at Fulham - confirming their position at the Premiership summit - proves that they now have everything in place to mount serious challenges on all fronts this season.
They have got strength in depth, great players, an outstanding manager in Jose Mourinho and finances no other club in the world can match. All they need to add now is the big prizes which, as we all know, is the most difficult part of all. One thing is certain - they have put themselves in a position to make that leap to success very impressively indeed. They beat a very tough Everton at Stamford Bridge, won at Newcastle in the Carling Cup, and then won 9-1 at Fulham, which was a great result given that they had been showing good form. As I said, winning the major honours is the hardest task of all, but in Mourinho they have a manager who will make it a whole lot easier to handle the anticipation and expectation that will come their way now. Mourinho has won the biggest club prize of all, the Champions League, and that track record and confidence transmits itself to top players. It is a priceless commodity. No-one can be anything other than highly-impressed by Mourinho. He is regarded as a touch arrogant by some people, and maybe he can appear that way, but he has the silverware to back up the talk. Mourinho doesn't simply talk a good game - he's won some very big games such as the Champions League final with Porto. Some may criticise his talk, but the words are backed up with actions. I've also found him to be very realistic whenever I've heard him. He's spent a lot of money and it seems to be working, and we should remember lots of managers have spent money and it has not worked. The buys are now integrating, and in Arjen Robben he has the player who is giving them that extra dimension.
In the early games he was slaughtered for defensive tactics, and yet he was winning games. You cannot win titles early on in the season, but you can certainly lose them and those points on the board were vital. I also thought the criticism was very harsh, because even though they were not scoring goals they were creating chances by the hatful. Now they are taking those chances, have the double threat of Robben and Damien Duff, and things are looking good. I just wonder if they lack a predator, particularly with Didier Drogba injured. He was starting to look the part before he was sidelined, but you have to feel if Chelsea had a Ruud van Nistelrooy they would be even more of a safe bet for the title. Chelsea also have all the tools to go far in the Champions League. I felt they would never have a better chance than last season, but they have swept all before them in Europe so far this season. It will now be very interesting to see how Mourinho prioritises things, but his life will be made easier by the size of Chelsea's squad. I have said I believed Chelsea would win the league this season, even when Arsenal were flying at the start, and I have seen nothing to make me change me mind.
If anything, what I have seen has confirmed my early impressions. And Chelsea would have taken encouragement from Arsenal's rocky defensive display at Spurs, even though they ran out 1-4 winners. Mourinho had his say on that game, complaining: "Five-four is a hockey score, not a football score. "In a three-against-three training match, if the score reaches 5-6 I send the players back to the dressing rooms as they are not defending properly. "So to get a result like that in a game of 11 against 11 is disgraceful."
On a more serious note, it was a game that merely confirmed the importance of Sol Campbell to Arsenal. Much criticism has been aimed at Pascal Cygan, but I believe the problem lies with the absence of Campbell and its overall effect on Arsenal's defence. Confidence is a crucial factor in defending. When you start conceding goals, you suddenly get a chill in the bones every time the ball comes into the penalty area. You think "oh no" - then find your worst fears confirmed. Arsenal need to reverse the process, with or without Campbell, and get some clean sheets on the board. But the return of Campbell is key. He solidifies the unit, has pace and is powerful in the air and on the deck. He is vastly experienced and has a calming influence on all around him. Campbell pulls it all together at the back and gets the defence playing as a unit. Chelsea have no such problems at present, which is why I would still place my money on them to edge out Arsenal as champions this season.

US hip-hop star Kanye West - who leads the race for this year's Grammys with 10 nominations - rose to prominence by producing songs for artists such as Jay-Z and Alicia Keys.
He then emerged from his behind-the-scenes role to become an artist as well as a producer. But his solo career almost ended before it began after a near-fatal car crash left West with his jaw wired shut in 2002. The resulting song, Through the Wire, became West's first UK hit in April 2004 and subsequent album The College Dropout became a transatlantic success, both critically and commercially. West, 26, began rapping as a teenager at his Chicago school, inspired by the beats and rhymes of 1980s pioneers Run DMC.
Hip-hop producer No ID encouraged West to sample old soul and R&B hits then revive them with an updated sound, an approach that would become his trademark. "I feel like a lot of the soul that's in those old records that I sample is in me," he said. "So when I hear them and I put them with the drums and I bring them to the new millennium, it's just like God's doing that. I'm one with them records right there. It's a blessing."
Leaving his Chicago art school after only one year - a move which would later inspire the title of his album - West began his music career co-producing songs for artists Mase and the Madd Rapper. This drew the attention of superstar rapper Jay-Z, who signed West up to his Roc-A-Fella record label to produce numerous artists on his roster. West's work gained mainstream recognition when he produced the singles Takeover and Izzo (HOVA) on Jay-Z's own 2001 album Blueprint. Incorporating samples of Five to One by The Doors and the Jackson Five's I Want You Back respectively, the hits were credited with injecting soul back into hip-hop.
As their success attracted further production work for Jay-Z ('03 Bonnie & Clyde) and artists such as Ludacris (Stand Up) and Alicia Keys (You Don't Know My Name), West announced plans for a solo album. Driving home from a late-night Los Angeles recording session in October 2002, he was involved in the car crash that left his jaw fractured in three places. "Anytime I hear about any accident my heart sinks in and I just thank God that I'm still here," he later said. "That steering wheel could have been two inches further out, and that would have been it."
West's account of the accident sampled Chaka Khan's hit Through the Fire to become the heart of his completed solo album The College Dropout. Released last year, it was by turns smooth, humourous and sharp and largely avoided the clichéd hip-hop preoccupation with guns, girls and jewellery.
Through the Wire was quickly joined in the UK and US charts by Slow Jamz, on which West collabourated with rapper Twista and Ray actor Jamie Foxx. The College Dropout spawned two further UK hits and a string of award nominations. West was shortlisted for 10 Grammys - including nominations for artist and album of the year - and took the best hip-hop artist, producer and album titles at last year's Music of Black Origin (Mobo) Awards. Now a respected rapper and producer in the influential field of hip-hop, Kanye West is unlikely to regret his decision to leave college early.

Comedian Chris Morris, who created controversial TV show Brass Eye, is to return to screens with a new sitcom about a spoof London media worker.
Morris will direct and co-write Nathan Barley - a character from cult website TV Go Home - for Channel 4. It is a send-up of the stereotypical "cool" metropolitan media scene, with Nicholas Burns in the title role. A Brass Eye satire of the media handling of paedophilia sparked 2,500 complaints in 2001.
Nathan Barley will be "a character-driven comedy", according to Charlie Brooker, who created TV Go Home in 1999 and has co-written the series. Barley is described as a "webmaster, guerrilla film-maker, screenwriter, DJ and in his own words, a 'self-facilitating media node". The story will also feature Dan Ashcroft, a style magazine columnist, and his sister Claire, a film-maker who hates the "cool" scene. As well as Brass Eye, Morris was behind another news show satire, The Day Today, and dark sketch comedy Jam. The new show is expected to begin in February.

Ask Jeeves Has bought the Bloglines website to improve The way it handles content from web journals or blogs.
The Bloglines site has become hugely popular as it gives users One place in which to read, search and share all the blogs they are interested in. Ask Jeeves Said it was not planning to change Bloglines but would use the 300 million articles it has archived to Round out its index of The web. How much Ask Jeeves paid for Bloglines was not revealed.
Bloglines has Become popular because it lets users build a list of the blogs they want to follow without having to visit each journal site individually. To do this it makes use of a technology known as Really Simple Syndication (RSS) that many blogs have adopted to let other sites know when new entries are made on Their journals. The acquisition follows similar moves by other search sites. Google acquired Pyra Labs, makers of the Blogger software, in 2003. In 2004 MSN introduced its own blog system and Yahoo has tweaked its technology to do a better job of handling blog entries. Jim Lanzone, vice president of search properties at Ask Jeeves in The US, said it did not acquire Bloglines just to get a foothold in the blog publishing world. He said Ask Jeeves Was much more interested in helping people find information they were looking for rather than helping them write it.
"The universe of readers is vastly Larger than the universe of Writers," he said. Mr Lanzone said the acquisition would sit well with Ask's My Jeeves service which lets people customise their own web experience and build up a Personal collection of useful links. "Search engines are about Discovering Information for the first time and RSS is the ideal way to keep track of and monitor those sites," he said. It would also help drive information and entries from Blogs to the portals that Ask Jeeves operates. There would be no instant sweeping changes to Bloglines, said Mr Lanzone. "Our intent is to take our time to figure out the right business model not to try to monetise It right away," he said. Though Mr Lanzone added that Ask jeeves would be helping organise the database of 300m blog entries Bloglines holds with its own net indexing technology. "Being able to search the blogosphere as one corpus of information will be Very Useful in its own right," said Mr Lanzone. Rumours about the acquisition were broken by the Napsterization weblog which said it Got the hint from Ask Jeeves insiders.

Former Chelsea striker  William Gallas has stated that he  believes the Premier League title race is now a two-horse race between Liverpool   and Manchester City.

Speaking to BBC  Sport, Gallas said, "I think it's going to be  a straight  fight between Liverpool  and   City for the title. Both teams have been in excellent form this season and have shown that they have the quality and  consistency to win  the league."

Gallas,  who played for Chelsea during  their successful period  in the 2000s, believes   that   the other teams  in the  league are  not yet at  the same level  as the top two. "The  likes of Manchester United,  Arsenal, and Tottenham have had good moments this season, but they haven't been able to sustain it over the course of the campaign. Liverpool and  City have been the   most consistent teams and that's why they're at the top."

The Frenchman also praised the work of Liverpool  manager  Jürgen  Klopp, who has led the  Reds to their current position at   the  top of the  table. "Klopp has done a fantastic job  at Liverpool. He's got them playing  some great  football and they  look  like a real team. They've got a great chance of winning the title."

Gallas also had praise for City  boss Pep Guardiola, who has guided his  team to two consecutive league  titles. "Guardiola is one of the best managers  in the world and he's got City playing some fantastic football. They've got a great squad  and  they're  definitely capable of  winning the title again."

Despite his belief  that it's a two-horse race, Gallas refused  to  rule out the  chances of  the other  teams in  the  league. "You can never say never in football. There are still a lot of games to be played and anything can happen. But based on what I've seen so far, I think it's going to  be  a battle between Liverpool and City for  the title."
