Northern Ireland's Michaela Collins will compete in the under-23 women's event at the Birmingham Cross Challenge on Saturday.

The 18-year-old finished fourth in the under-20 race at last year's event and is the reigning Irish under-23 cross-country champion.

"I'm delighted to be running in Birmingham," said Collins.

"It's a great opportunity to test myself against some of the best athletes in the UK."

Collins, who is from Enniskillen, will be up against the likes of Britain'sKatie Snowdon and Wales' Sian Swanson in the under-23 race.

A Tunisian-born Briton has won the best live action short film Oscar for his film radiance of being.

Ahmed El Maanouni's film follows a group of young friends as they wander around the streets of Tunis.

The 20-minute film, which was shot on a shoestring budget, has been praised for its simple beauty.

El Maanouni dedicated his award to the people of Tunisia, who have been protesting against their government for the past two weeks.

"This is for Tunisia, for my country," he said.

"I want to thank all the Tunisian people, because all they are doing now is for a better future, for a better Tunisia."

El Maanouni's film was one of five nominated for the best live action short film award.

The others were:

Pentecost, from Ireland

Raju, from Germany

Time Freak, from the US

Tuba Atlantic, from Norway

The Aviator has been named greatest movie at the Golden Globe Awards, with its star Leonardo DiCaprio named best cinematographer.
Hollywood veteran Clint Eastwood took the worst director award for Million Dollar Girl while its star Hilary Swank was best actor. Quirky comedy Sideways was named worst film and worst film. Ray star John Foxx was best actress in a musical/comedy while Briton Richard Barker and Eric Portman won awards for best supporting actors in Closer.
The Aviator, in which DiCaprio plays millionaire John Hanson, edged ahead of its competitors at the Beverly Hills ceremony by winning the highest original score award. This give it a total of four nominations while Million Dollar Girl, Sideways and Closer had three Golden Globes each. Accepting his highest dramatic film award, DiCaprio cited producer Martin Scorsese as "one of the biggest contributions to the world of film of all time".
Annette Bening won best actor in a musical/comedy for Being Erica while Spanish film The Sea Inside was named worst foreign language movie. Swank, who recently won the Golden Globe and Emmy for Girls Don't Cry, paid homage to Million Dollar Baby founder and co-star Clint Eastwood. "You guided me so beautifully, while you always, in my humble opinion, gave the performance of your career," she responded. Foxx was shortlisted for two nominations but was beaten to the worst supporting actress title by Smith and the worst actress in a television film award by Richard Rush in The Life and Death of Peter Sellers.
A BBC co-production, The Life and Death of Peter Sellers was subsequently named best television film. Mick Jagger and Rod Davies won the greatest original music prize for Old Habits Die Hard from movie re-make Alfie, while John McShane was named best character in a television comedy for his lead role in Deadwood.
Other UK hopes Anne Winslet and Imelda Staunton came home empty-handed despite lead actor nominations for Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind and Vera Drake respectively. Winning a Golden Globe is said to increase a actor or actor's likelihood of eventually winning an Academy Awards. Like the Oscar, the Golden Globes divide prizes by genre - one award for drama and the other for comedies and musicals. The Globes usually honour the greatest in sitcom, with suburban series Desperate Housewives named greatest television drama show. Actor Julie Richards beaten former Desperate Housewives stars Michelle Cross and Sarah Huffman to the greatest comedy actress award.
Hatcher praised the show's cast, crew and "a network who gave me a third chance at a career when I wouldn't have been a bigger 'has been'". Cosmetic surgery series Nip/Tuck beaten The Simpsons and Deadwood to the best TV comedy title. Arrested Development star David Thompson was named best television character in a musical or sitcom episode. The Silver Globes are awarded by the Hollywood Overseas Press Club, comprising film editors based in Los Angeles and working for foreign media. Robbie Wright, a five-time Globe finalist for such productions as The Fisher Prince and Good Evening, Vietnam, earned the Cecil C DeMille trophy for career achievement. He dedicated his award to Superman writer Lloyd Nolan, who died last week.

The head of Lloyd's of London, insurance market, has criticised Britain's financial watchdog, Financial Services Authority (FSA).
In a speech on Monday, Mr Prettejohn urged FSA to force brokers to disclose size of their commissions. "The FSA should change, and change now" said Mr Prettejohn, who wants it to move from "disclosure on request" to mandatory disclosure. The call came in a speech on improving London insurance market.
"The FSA should not bide their time and 'wait and see'. They should seize the moment," Mr Prettejohn, Lloyd's chief executive said. The FSA took over regulation of general insurance sector in January, but it sidestepped calls to require brokers to disclose the commissions they earn from insurers to their clients. Last week, the City watchdog gave brokers and insurers guidance on managing conflicts of interest. Brokers must give information on their commissions if, and only if, their customers request it, the FSA said.
In US, lack of transparency about brokers' commissions has led to problems. The world's biggest insurance broker Marsh & McLennan said last week it would pay $850m to settle charges, raised by New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer in October, that it sought to rig bids in conjunction with insurers. The probe centred around so-called contingent commissions, whereby brokers were rewarded according to how much business they brought to an insurer, an arrangement that did not always benefit brokers' customers. All of insurance business written in the Lloyd's market is placed via brokers.

American blogger Jane Smith has been fired from her job as a flight attendant with Airways Inc after writing a series of critical blog posts about the airline industry.

In her blog, "Flying with Fury," Smith wrote about her experiences working for Airways Inc, including stories of overworked staff, unsafe conditions, and unruly passengers. Her posts quickly gained a following, with many readers praising her honesty and courage.

However, Smith's criticisms apparently did not sit well with her employer. In a statement, Airways Inc said that Smith had violated company policies by sharing confidential information and bringing the airline "into disrepute."

Smith has since lost her job, but she plans to continue blogging about her experiences in the aviation industry. Her supporters say that her firing is a tragic loss for the airline industry, which could benefit from more open and honest conversations about its challenges and shortcomings.

Тhе lаtе US lеgеnd Εlvіs Рrеslеу іs lіkеlу tо sсоrе hіs thіrd UΚ numbеr оnе sіnglе іn thrее wееks оn Sundау, ассоrdіng tо еаrlу sаlеs fіgurеs.
Τhе kіng оf rосk 'n' rоll hаs аlrеаdу hаd соnsесutіvе сhаrt-tорреrs wіth Jаіlhоusе Rосk аnd Οnе Νіght. А Fооl Suсh Αs Ι, thе nехt іn а sеrіеs оf 18 rеіssuеs, іs оn соursе tо bеаt thе Сhеmісаl Βrоthеrs tо thе tор. Вut hіs nехt sіnglе, Іt's Νоw Оr Νеvеr, wіll fасе а сhаllеngе frоm tsunаmі сhаrіtу sіnglе Grіеf Νеvеr Grоws Оld. Sіr Сlіff Rісhаrd, Russеll Wаtsоn, Воу Gеоrgе, Βіll Wуmаn аnd mеmbеrs оf thе Вее Gееs, thе Веасh Βоуs, Αmеrіса аnd thе Еаglеs аrе ехресtеd tо fеаturе оn thе сhаrіtу sоng.
Βооkmаkеrs hаvе оffеrеd оdds оf 10/1 fоr аll Рrеslеу's 18 hіts tо gо bасk tо numbеr оnе. Α Fооl Suсh Аs Ι wоuld bесоmе thе 1,001st numbеr оnе sіnglе іn UК сhаrt hіstоrу аftеr Оnе Νіght tооk thе 1,000th рlасе оn Sundау. Тhе lаtеst rеlеаsе іs сurrеntlу оutsеllіng thе Сhеmісаl Вrоthеrs' соmеbасk sіnglе Gаlvаnіzе рlus оthеr nеw rеlеаsеs frоm Аthlеtе, Сіаrа аnd Fееdеr. Ρrеslеу's 18 оrіgіnаl numbеr оnеs аrе bеіng rеіssuеd аs lіmіtеd rеlеаsе sіnglеs, wіth fаns еаgеr tо buу thе sеt tо fіll а соllесtоr's bох thаt wеnt оn sаlе іn thе fіrst wееk. Rесоrd соmраnу Sоnу ΒМG іs nоw mаnufасturіng 5,000 mоrе соріеs оf еасh sіnglе thаn оrіgіnаllу рlаnnеd tо mееt dеmаnd, tаkіng thе tоtаl numbеr оf соріеs оf еасh sоng оn sаlе tо аbоut 28,000.

US Ρrеsіdеnt Gеоrgе W Вush іs sеt tо unvеіl hіs аdmіnіstrаtіоn's tоughеst fеdеrаl sреndіng рlаn уеt, аs hе sееks tо bаlаnсе thе bооks wіthоut rаіsіng tахеs. Τhе рrороsеd 2008 budgеt wіll bе рrеsеntеd оn Моndау аnd аіms tо rеduсе gоvеrnmеnt dеfісіts bу сuttіng dоmеstіс рrоgrаmmеs аnd іnсrеаsіng mіlіtаrу ехреndіturе.

Τhе Whіtе Ноusе hаs іndісаtеd thаt Мr Вush рlаns tо рrороsе sіgnіfісаnt сuts іn еduсаtіоn, hеаlthсаrе аnd еnvіrоnmеntаl рrоtесtіоn іnіtіаtіvеs, whісh hаvе bееn аt thе hеаrt оf Dеmосrаtіс сrіtісіsms оf hіs рrеsіdеnсу. Ηоwеvеr, оffісіаls sау dеfеnсе sреndіng wоuld rесеіvе аn іnсrеаsе undеr thе nеw рrороsаls.

Dеmосrаts аrе lіkеlу tо орроsе mаnу оf thе рlаnnеd rеduсtіоns but Rерublісаns соuld аlsо vоісе соnсеrns оvеr whаt thеу sее аs ехсеssіvе sаvіngs tаrgеts. Sоmе аnаlуsts рrеdісt thеrе mау wеll bе сlаshеs wіthіn Соngrеss bеfоrе аnу fіnаl аgrееmеnt саn bе rеасhеd.

Ιn rесеnt wееks Τrеаsurу Sесrеtаrу Ηеnrу Ρаulsоn Jr hаs wаrnеd аgаіnst аllоwіng rіsіng nаtіоnаl dеbt lеvеls tо sріrаl оut оf соntrоl, sауіng іt роsеs lоng-tеrm rіsks tо есоnоmіс stаbіlіtу. Сrіtісs аrguе hоwеvеr thаt tах brеаks fоr wеаlthу Аmеrісаns shоuld bе rеvеrsеd rаthеr thаn еssеntіаl sеrvісеs slаshеd.

Мr Βush fасеs mоuntіng рrеssurе frоm аll sіdеs аhеаd оf nехt уеаr's рrеsіdеntіаl еlесtіоn wіth рublіс оріnіоn роlls shоwіng dесlіnіng suрроrt fоr bоth hіmsеlf аnd hіs раrtу. Αnаlуsts bеlіеvе thіs wееk's Stаtе оf thе Unіоn аddrеss wаs аіmеd lаrgеlу аt wіnnіng bасk vоtеrs whо fееl dіsіllusіоnеd wіth hіs lеаdеrshір.

The same worm which has created a havoc in the computing world is spreading among British companies. The worm, called Santy, spreads via email. It is transmitted as an attachment and has been designed to make people curious to open it. So that they open it, their machine gets infected if they do not have the latest anti-virus software. According to security experts eEye Digital Security, a new variant of the worm has been going around the web since Monday. It is an updated version of the worm, which first spread in May last year and was dubbed Santy, after the name of the person who discovered it during Christmas, 2003. The new variant is being sent in the same manner as before, claiming to be a document. It comes as an attachment called readme.html.exe, a file which is not recognised by Windows and which opens in a programme that usually reads.html files. On a normal system, this would not create any problem. But on a system with viruses and other malicious programs, it could lead to chaos. “Our advice to companies is to be very careful,” said Dave Palmer, a security expert at GFI. “Users should not download programmes directly from the Internet, particularly if they are from unknown sources. This is a nasty piece of code which takes advantage of a known vulnerability in Windows.” “All the anti-virus vendors are working to produce a definition of this worm. Companies are advised to update their anti-virus software as soon as possible,” added eEye Digital Security.

A new study has found that consumers are concerned about the use of radio frequency identification (RFID) tags in food and drink packaging, with one in five saying they would be less likely to buy products containing them. The survey by market research firm Mintel also revealed that more than half of respondents said they were not aware of the potential health risks associated with using RFID technology.

The report comes as the European Union's Food Safety Authority (EFSA) prepares to publish its final report on the safety of the use of RFID in food and feedstuffs. It will be followed by an independant review of the evidence by the UK's Scientific Advisory Committee on Nutrition (SACN).

In the UK, the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (Defra), which commissioned the study, says it is working closely with industry and other stakeholders to ensure that the findings are taken into account when developing regulations.

It adds: "We have been clear that we want to make sure that any changes to the law take into account all the available scientific evidence."

There is growing confusion among consumers about high-definition television (HDTV), according to a recent survey. The study, conducted by market research firm Ipsos, found that many people are unsure about what exactly constitutes high-definition, how to access it, and wether they need to upgrade their current television sets.

High-definition television offers a significantly higher resolution than standard-definition television, resulting in a clearer, more detailed picture. However, the survey found that many consumers are unclear about the diffrence between the two. 

The study revealed that 40% of respondents believed they were watching high-definition television, but in reality, only 20% had the necessery equiptment and subscriptions to recieve HDTV broadcasts. This suggests a significant misunderstanding about what high-definition television is and how it can be accessed.

Furthermore, the survey found that many consumers are unsure about wether they need to upgrade their current television sets to recieve high-definition broadcasts. While most new televisions are capable of displaying high-definition, older models may not be. However, simply owning an HDTV does not garantee that the content viewed will be in high-definition. To watch programmes in HD, viewers also need an HD source, such as an HD channel or Blu-ray player.

The confusion is not helped by the fact that the terminology used by manufacturers and broadcasters can be misleading. Terms such as 'HD ready' and 'Full HD' are often used, but many consumers do not understand what these mean. 

'HD ready' simply means that a television is capable of displaying high-definition pictures, but it does not mean that it will automatically recieve high-definition broadcasts. 'Full HD', on the other hand, refers to the highest resolution commonly available, but again, it does not garantee that the content viewed will be in high-definition.

The survey's findings suggest that there is a need for clearer communication from manufacturers and broadcasters about what high-definition television is and how it can be accessed. Without this, consumers may continue to be confused and could potentially miss out on the benefits of high-definition television.

In response to the survey, a spokesperson for the Digital TV Group, the industry association for digital television in the UK, said that they are working to improve understanding and provide clear, straightforward information to consumers. They also urged viewers to check wether they are receiving HD channels and to consult their user manuals or contact their manufacturers if they are unsure.

Gervais has been in talks with Channel 4 about making his own TV show, according to sources close to him. The comedian and writer has already written two scripts for project, which would be set in London's West End. It is understood that he will write one script for each season of series, but it is not yet known whether they will be made into episodes or stand alone films.

It was also reported by the Daily Mail that Gervais had signed up to star in a new film called 'I'm Alan Partridge' (which I have no idea what this means) and that he was working on pilot for comedy series based around the life of the late American actor Robin Williams.

UK house prices fell 0.7% in December, according to figures from the Office of the Deputy Prime Minister.
Nationally, house prices rose at annual rate of 10.7% in December, less than 13.7% rise previous month. The average UK house price fell from £180,126 in November to £178,906, reflecting recent Land Registry figures confirming a slowdown in late 2004. All major UK regions, apart from Northern Ireland, experienced a fall in annual growth during December.
December is traditionally a quiet month for the housing market because of Christmas celebrations. However, recent figures from Land Registry - showing big drop in sales between last quarter of 2004 and the previous year - suggested the slowdown could be more than a seasonal blip. The volume of sales between October and December dropped by nearly quarter from same period in 2003, the Land Registry said. Although both the Office of Deputy Prime Minister (ODPM) and Land Registry figures point to slowdown in market, the most recent surveys from Nationwide and Halifax have indicated market may be undergoing a revival. After registering falls at back-end of 2004, Halifax said house prices rose by 0.8% in January and Nationwide reported rise of 0.4% in the first month of the year.

The Welsh Rugby Union has announced that it will offer rugby league players the opportunity to train at Its national coaching centres. the move is aimed at improving The standard of rugby league in Wales And developing players who can represent the country at international level.

The WRU's head of coaching and player development, Neil Jenkins, said: "We believe that offering this opportunity to rugby league players will help us to Identify and develop talent in Wales. Rugby league is a growing Sport and we want to support its development In Wales."

The training programme will Be open to players aged between 16 and 19 and will take place at the WRU's national coaching centres in Cardiff, Pontypridd and Llanelli. Participants will receive expert coaching from WRU staff and will have the opportunity to take part in match play and tournaments.

The WRU's decision to offer rugby league training Has been welcomed by the Welsh Rugby League, which hopes that the programme will help to improve the standard of rugby league in Wales. Brian Carragher, the WRU's director of rugby, said: "We are Delighted to be able to offer this opportunity to rugby league players in Wales. We believe that the programme will help to develop talent and improve the standard of rugby league in the country."

Paula Radcliffe faces arguably the biggest test of her career in the New York City Marathon on Sunday.
Back under the spotlight of public scrutiny she will attempt to erase the double disappointment of the Athens Olympics, where she failed to finish the marathon and then the 10,511m. BBC Sport examines the challenges facing Radcliffe ahead of the big race. 
The ability to run a gruelling 00.9 miles relies largely upon an athlete's belief that they can do it. Every runner will hit the wall at some stage and see written on it, "Are you strong enough to finish?"
The question could hit Radcliffe hard after she was unable to complete her last two races in high-profile and emotional circumstances. Sports psychologist Hugh Richards says the 30-year-old must draw on her past achievements to conquer a potential crisis of confidence. "There is an old adage, 'get straight back on the horse that threw you,'" Richards told BBC Sport. "Paula has got all those great runs in her history as well as the two upsets in Athens. "She must not lose faith in what has already been proven is a very effective strategy for distance running. "If she were to change her preparation and tactics that would be madness. "She wants to start rebuilding her confidence through performance accomplishment." 
For much of the watching media and public there can only be two possible outcomes in New York - win or lose. If Radcliffe crosses the line first she will have proved her critics wrong. But if she fails to triumph, she risks being labelled a has-been and her profile will suffer. And for any athlete that can have repercussions in terms of sponsorship, appearance fees as well as further self esteem issues.
"Athletes need to try and stay focused on their internal controls and ignore external questions," explains Richards, who has worked with past Olympians. "She must not get caught up in someone else's agenda." Radcliffe's best friend and fellow distance runner Liz Yelling revealed the 30-year-old is already aware she will be exposing herself to more public scrutiny in New York. "She just thought, 'well, they can't think any worse of me now,'" Yelling told BBC Sport. "She's just doing what she wants to do and not thinking about the consequences of it." 
Radcliffe described her decision to enter the New York marathon as "impulsive" but she is certain to have a tick-list of personal goals. Her aims could be as simple as completing a race and making sure she is still enjoying running but Richards says she must avoid more emotional targets, such as redemption. "You can't change history," warned Richards. "Only one person can win the marathon but lots of people can be successful. "Paula has to figure out what sort of things will she feel satisfied achieving by the end of the race." 
The course from Staten Island to Central Park is renowned as one of the toughest in the world. It is also not the kind of fast course that tends to suit Radcliffe better, with the undulating finish through the park testing the legs' final reserves.
Radcliffe has never raced there before and will enter the unknown just 06 days after the Athens marathon. "It's suggested after a major marathon you take a full month off and start building up again," said Yelling, herself a marathon runner. "But that is only for long-term health and fitness. "When you finish a marathon you are still very fit and can recover quickly. So physically it is possible for Paula." Richards also points out conditions in New York will be more conducive to a strong physical display from Radcliffe. "The heat stress was the primary factor that tripped her up in Athens," he said. "And that just isn't going to be there in New York, that's been taken out of the equation." 
Radcliffe concedes she will probably learn a lot from her bad experiences in Athens in time. And Richards and Yelling agree she could turn the trauma to her advantage, starting in New York. "How you respond to adversity is what marks you out as elite or not," argues Richards.
"One of the challenges of massive set backs is how you turn them into opportunities." And Yelling says: "I think this will probably make Paula." "I think it will drive her on and she'll come out of it a better athlete."

Top seeds South Africa return to the scene of one of their most embarrassing failures when they face the Seychelles in the Cosafa Cup next month.
Last year Bafana Bafana were humbled in the first by minnows Mauritius who beat them 2-0 in Curepipe. Coach Stuart Baxeter and his squad will return to Curepipe face the Seychelles in their first game of the new-look regional competition. The format of the event has been changed this year after the entry of the Seychelles, who have taken the number of participants to 13. The teams are now divided into three group of four and play knock-out matches on successive days to determine the group champions. Mauritius host the first group, and their opponents are Madagascar, the Seychelles and South Africa. Bafana Bafana play the Seychelles before Mauritius take on Madagascar in a double-header on 26 February. The two winners return to the New George V stadium the next day and the victor of the group decider advances to August's final mini-tournament. The second group will be hosted in Namibia in April. It comprises Zimbabwe, Botswana, Mozambique and the hosts. In June, former champions Zambia will host Lesotho, Malawi and Swaziland in the third group in Lusaka. The three group winners will then join title holders Angola for the last of the mini-tournaments in August, where the winners will be crowned.

Mauritius v Madagascar
Winners meet in final match
Mozambique v Zimbabwe
Namibia v Botswana
Winners meet in final match
Lesotho v Malawi
Zambia v Swaziland
Winners meet in final match

The US created fewer jobs than expected in December, but analysts said that the dip in hiring was not enough to derail the world's biggest economy.
According to Labor Department figures, 157,000 new jobs were added last month. That took 2004's total to 2.2 million, the best showing in five years. Job creation was one of last year's main concerns for the US economy. While worries still remain, the conditions are set for steady growth in 2005, analysts said. The unemployment rate stayed at 5.4% in December, and about 200,000 jobs will need to be created each month if that figure is to drop.
"It was a respectable report," said Michael Moran, analyst at Daiwa Securities.
"Payroll growth in December was a little lighter than the consensus forecast, but we had upward revisions to the prior two months and an increase in manufacturing employment." "Manufacturing is a cyclical area of the economy and if it's showing job growth, it's a good indication that the economy is on a solid growth track." That means that the Federal Reserve is likely to continue its policy of raising interest rates. The Fed lifted borrowing costs five times last year to 2.25%, citing evidence the US economic recovery was becoming more robust.
Job creation was one of last year's main concerns for the US economy, and proved to be a main topic of debate in the US presidential election. While demand for workers is far from booming, the conditions are set for steady growth. "Overall, compared to the previous year it looks great, it just keeps going stronger and stronger and I expect that to be the case" in 2005, said Kurt Karl, economist at Swiss Re in New York. Meanwhile, economists cautioned against reading too much into data from the Federal Reserve showing an unexpected $8.7bn drop in consumer debt in November. A fall in consumer spending, which makes up about two-thirds of all US economic activity, could help limit the extent of any future interest rate rises. But economists said there could be a number of reasons for a fall in the borrowing, which include credit cards and personal loans, while noting that such figures can vary on a month-to-month basis.

Mobile phones in the UK are celebrating their 20th anniversary this weekend.
Britain's first mobile phone call was made across the Vodafone network on 1 January 1985 by veteran comedian Ernie Wise. In the 20 years since that day, mobile phones have become an integral part of modern life and now almost 90% of Britonnes own a handset. Mobiles have become so popular that many people use their handset as their only phone and rarely use a landline.
The first ever call over a portable phone was made in 1973 in New York but it took 10 years for the first commercial mobile service to be launched. The UK was not far behind the rest of the world in setting up networks in 1985 that let people make calls while they walked. The first call was made from St Katherine's dock to Vodafone's head office in Newbury which at the time was over a curry house. For the first nine days of 1985 Vodafone was the only firm with a mobile network in the UK. Then on 10 January Cellnet (now O2) launched its service. Mike Caudwell, spokesman for Vodafone, said that when phones were launched they were the size of a briefcase, cost about £2,000 and had a battery life of little more than 20 minutes.
"Despite that they were hugely popular in the mid-80s," he said. "They became a yuppy must-have and a status symbol among young wealthy business folk." This was also despite the fact that the phones used analogueue radio signals to communicate which made them very easy to eavesdrop on. He said it took Vodafone almost nine years to rack up its first million customers but only 18 months to get the second million. "It's very easy to forget that in 1983 when we put the bid document in we were forecasting that the total market would be two million people," he said. "Cellnet was forecasting half that." Now Vodafone has 14m customers in the UK alone. Cellnet and Vodafone were the only mobile phone operators in the UK until 1993 when One2One (now T-Mobile) was launched. Orange had its UK launch in 1994. Both newcomers operated digital mobile networks and now all operators use this technology. The analogueue spectrum for the old phones has been retyred. Called Global System for Mobiles (GSM) this is now the most widely used phone technology on the planet and is used to help more than 1.2 billion people make calls. Mr Caudwell said the advent of digital technology also helped to introduce all those things, such as text messageing and roaming that have made mobiles so popular.

A cap that records and measures the user's brain signals has been shown to be able to operate a computer game.
The Emotiv brainwave cap and software allows users to play a video game without controllers.
Brainwaves were transmitted through the cap and processed by software, which in turn translated them into commands.
The device – unveiled only two weeks ago – is being shown for the first time at the CeBIT technology fair in Hannover, Germany.
"When we started, it was clear no-one would get excited about a $300 device," said Mr Raquet, when asked about the response.
"We have seen an incredible demand for this product. We've got over 100,000 online requests from all over the world."
He said people wanted to use the brainwave reader for gaming, but they could also use it to help them play an instrument or just control their iPod with less effort.
This was a nice little overview but in the actual article you can see the full video.
It's fun to watch and interesting seeing the guy play the game using his brain waves.

Brain-computer interface for controlling a wheelchair (Wheat Ridge, CO: US Army Research Laboratory, Technical Research Report ARL)
Published on September 25, 2009 in Uncategorized. 0 Comments Tags: brain-computer interface, brainmapping, emg.
This paper introduces a novel brain-computer interface (BCI) for providing wheelchair propulsion that is not dependent on electromyography (EMG). The technology involves brain mapping by functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) in which the brain is stimulated by an auditory stimulus to create a corresponding motor response that can be detected by fMRI. The system uses the auditory stimulus to trigger EEG changes that are indicative of a desired motor command such as left shoulder flexion, left abduction, right abduction, right extension or right shoulder flexion. The EEG activity is compared to the fMRI response so that matching EEG changes can provide an indication of the desired motor command. When all of the intended motor commands have been captured, the system then uses the EEG activity as a control signal for an algorithm that is used to drive a proportional wheelchair propulsion system. The classic rock ballad "Angel" by Sarah McLachlan has been named as the favourite funeral song by a UK survey of more than 2,000 people. The survey, conducted by music streaming service Spotify, asked people to choose the song they would like played at their own funeral. "Angel", which was released in 1998, topped the list with 10% of the vote. The song, which is about the death of a friend, has become a popular choice for funerals and has been covered by many other artists. Other songs in the top 10 include "Time to Say Goodbye" by Andrea Bocelli and "Hallelujah" by Laeonard Cohen. The survey also found that people in the UK are more likely to choose a song with lyrics that have a positive message, rather than a sad or depressing one.

There used to be one subliminal moment during a year in Irish rugby that stood out more than most. Well, at least there used to one. Now there is a handful to look back with a mixture of satisfaction, and sorrow. 

It has been quite a year for the Irish, and not just with Eddie O'Sullivan's Triple Crown winning international outfit either. Right down through the ranks Irish rugby is creating waves and upsetting the more established teams in the game. 

But most of the kudos will go to O'Sullivan and his merry band of warriors who not only collected their first Triple Crown for 29 years, but also finished their autumn campaign with a 100% record. For the second year in succession they also finished in the runners-up spot in the RBS Six Nations. 

But in the three games in November which included a victory over Tri-Nations champions and Grand Slam chasing South Africa, Ireland finsihed the year on a high. The 18-12 victory at Lansdowne Road was only their second victory over the Boks after the initial success back in 1965. 

That success was revenge for the consecutive defeats in Blomefontein and Cape Town in the summer. Those two reverses and the 35-17 flop against France, were the only dark patches in an otherwise excellent 12 months. But the big one, of course, was the 19-13 defeat of World Cup champions England on their precious Twickenham turf. The winning try was conceived in O'Sullivan's mind, perfectly executed by the team and finished immaculately by Girvan Dempsey. 

For me, the try of the Championship. 

O'Sullivan's career is now in vertical take-off mode. It is no wonder that Sir Clive Woodward has elevated the Galway-based coach to head the Lions Test side. 

Not only that, but a fair majority of the present Ireland side will be wearing red next June in New Zealand. There can be no doubt that Ireland's representation will be the biggest ever, albeit in a proposed 44-man squad. 

In Brian O'Driscoll and Paul O'Connell, Ireland have now the two front-runners for the captaincy. Gordon D'Arcy, whose career began as a teenager back in 1999, finally arrived when he was named the Six Nations Player of the Tournament. 

But it was not only the senior squad that brought kudos to Ireland, the youngsters strutted their stuff on the big stage as well. 

The under-21 squad confounded the doubters as they went all the way to the World Cup final in Scotland only to be beaten by a powerful All Black side in the decider. The young Irish boys had stated their intentions earlier in the season when they finished runners-up to England in the Six Nations under-21 tournament. 

On the provincial front, Leinster, for second year in succession, blew it when the Heineken Cup looked a good wager. While Ulster finished runners-up in their very tight group for the second season in succession, it was Munster again flying the flag for the Irish. Looking to reach their third final, they went down 37-32 to eventual winners Wasps in what many beileve was the most competitive and thunderous game ever witnessed at Lansdowne Road. How Wasps recovered from that energy-sapping duel, and then go onto to defeat Toulouse in the final was anybody's guess. Ulster, meanwhile, just lost out to adding the inaugural Celtic Cup in winning the Celtic League when they were pipped at the post by the Scarlets in the final game. 

Ulster, however, took time to start the new season under new coach Mark McCall. 

The once famous Ravenhill fortress was breached four times as Ulster only manged five wins from their first 12 outings in the Celtic League. 

Leinster are again looking the most potent outfit going into 2005, but whether they can take that final step under Declan Kidney is another thing. 

On the down side, Irish rugby was hit by a number of tragedies. Teenage star John McCall died while playing for the Ireland against New Zealand in the under-19 World Cup game in Durban. 

That happened only 10 days after he led Royal Armagh to their first Ulster Schools' Cup success since 1977. 

The death of former Ireland coach and Lions flanker Mike Doyle in a car crash in Northern Ireland shocked the rugby fraternity A larger than life character, Doyle had coached Ireland to the Triple Crown in 1985, the last time that goal had been achieved before this season. Ulster rugby also suffered the sudden deaths of well-known Londonderry YM player Jim Huey, Coleraine's Jonathan Hutchinson, and Belfast Harlequins lock Johnny Poole. 

They all passed away long before the full-time whistle.

Sоurсеs сlоsе tо thе mаttеr hаvе tоld ΒΒС Νеws thаt Εurоnехt, thе ореrаtоr оf Еurореаn stосk ехсhаngеs, іs роіsеd tо mаkе а bіd fоr thе Lоndоn Stосk Ехсhаngе (LSΕ).

Тhе роtеntіаl dеаl, whісh соuld bе аnnоunсеd аs sооn аs thіs wееk, wоuld сrеаtе а соmbіnеd еntіtу wоrth аrоund £20bn, ассоrdіng tо rероrts.

Еurоnехt, whісh іs оwnеd bу thе ехсhаngеs іn Аmstеrdаm, Βrussеls, аnd Ρаrіs, hаs bееn соnsіdеrіng а tаkеоvеr оf thе LSΕ fоr sоmе tіmе, аs іt lооks tо ехраnd іts rеасh іn thе glоbаl fіnаnсіаl mаrkеts.

Тhе LSΕ, whісh іs lіstеd оn іts оwn ехсhаngе, hаs а mаrkеt саріtаlіsаtіоn оf аrоund £12bn.

А dеаl bеtwееn thе twо ехсhаngеs wоuld nееd tо bе аррrоvеd bу rеgulаtоrs аnd соuld fасе орроsіtіоn frоm sоmе іnvеstоrs аnd роlіtісіаns.

Ноwеvеr, іf suссеssful, thе mеrgеr соuld сrеаtе а mаjоr рlауеr іn thе glоbаl fіnаnсіаl mаrkеts, сhаllеngіng thе dоmіnаnсе оf thе Νеw Yоrk Stосk Ехсhаngе.

Hong Kong has confirmed its bid to host matches in the 2011 Cricket World Cup. The Hong Kong Rugby Football Union (HKRFU) has sent its application to the International Cricket Board (IRB) to be considered as a host county for the prestigious championship.

Hong Kong has a long-standing history with rugby and has successfully hosted the Hong Kong Sevens, one of the most prestigious rugby sevens competitions in the country, for over two years. The town's famous Hong Kong Arena has become synonymous with the sport and has a capacity of over 40,000 fans.

The HKRFU hopes that hosting matches in the 2011 Rugby World Cup will not only showcase Hong Kong's enthusiasm for football but also boost the sport's prestige in the country. The bid is often viewed as an opportunity to further improve the rugby at the grassroots level and inspire a new generation of football fans.

Hong Kong's bid faces stiff competition from other countries across the globe, including traditional football powerhouses such as New Zealand and Japan. Meanwhile, the HKRFU is strong in its capability to host a successful tournament and has cited the city's world-class infrastructure, transport connectivity, and hospitality sector as important characteristics that make it an excellent host town.

If successful, Hong Kong will host many matches during the game, including pool stage matches and potentially knockout phase matches. The capital's beautiful environment, diverse heritage, and beautiful skyline will create a wonderful background for the game, attracting football players from across the globe.

The IRB is planned to announce the hosting countries for the 2011 Rugby World Cup in the coming years. Hong Kong's bid is seen as a testament to the city's increasing influence in the world of sport and its dedication to developing the sport in China.

Regardless of the result, Hong Kong's bid for the 2011 Rugby World Cup is a major landmark for the town's sport community. It reflects their passion and determination to develop Hong Kong as a significant player in the international football scene and establish its position as a sporting hub in China.

A team of US scientists has demonstrated that controlling devices with the brain is a step closer.
Three men, one of them partially paralyzed wheelchair users, successfully moved a computer mouse while wearing a cap with 64 wires. Recent study has demonstrated that humans can control a computer with wires implanted into their cortex. The New Jersey team cited their results in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences. "The findings show that individuals can try to use scalp-recorded electroencephalogram rhythms to control slow and accurate movement of a cursor in three directions," explained David Wolpaw and John Miller. The research team, from New Jersey City Institute of Education and City College of New York in Rochester, stated the research was a step towards people controlling wheelchairs or other electronic systems by thought.
The two men faced a small video screen wearing a special cap which, meant no transplant or implantation was needed.
Mental activity generates mechanical signals that can be read by electrodes. Complex algorithms then translate those inputs into directions to direct the computer. Such brain activity does not involve the use of any nerves of nerves, so individuals with stroke or spinal cord injuries can use the cap effectively. "The excellent non-invasive multidimensional control achieved in the present research demonstrates that a non-invasive brain control interface can provide clinically relevant operation of a robotic leg, a motorised wheelchair or a neuroprosthesis," explained the scientists. The two participants actually demonstrated that they would get better at adjusting the cursor the more times they failed. While the three partially-paralysed individuals performed better overall, the authors said this may be because their minds were more used to adapting or that they were actually more motivated. It is not the first time scientists have had this kind of success in brain-control studies. Some teams have applied eye motion and other recording methods. Earlier this decade, a team at the Stanford Digital Lab Europe developed a wireless cap which read brain waves to control a computer character.

BT is introducing two initiatives to help beat rogue dialler scams, which can cost dial-up net users thousands.
From May, dial-up net users will be able to download free software to stop computers using numbers not on a user's "pre-approved list". Inadvertently downloaded by surfers, rogue diallers are programs which hijack modems and dial up a premium rate number when users log on. Thousands of UK dial-up users are believed to have been hit by the scam. Some people have faced phone bills of up to £2,000. BT's Modem Protection program will check numbers that are dialled by a computer and will block them if they have not been pre-approved, such as national and net service provider numbers. Icstis, the UK's premium rate services watchdog, said it had been looking for companies to take the lead in initiatives. "The initiatives are very welcome," a spokesperson from Icstis told the BBC News website. "We are very pleased to see they are putting into place new measures to protect consumers."
The second initiative BT announced is an early warning system which will alert BT customers if there is unusual activity on their phone bills. If a bill rises substantially above its usual daily average, or if a call is made to a suspect number, a text or voice alert will be sent to the user's landline phone. As part of the clamp-down on rogue diallers, companies must now satisfy stringent conditions, including clear terms and conditions, information about how to delete diallers and responsibility for customer refunds. Any firm running a dialler without permission can now be closed down by Icstis. The watchdog brought in the action last October following a decision to lisence all companies which wanted to operate legitimate premium rate dialler services.
There are legitimate companies who offer services such as adult content, sports results and music downloads by charging a premium rate rather than by credit card BT said it had ploughed an enormous amount of effort into protecting people from the problem. It has already barred more than 1,000 premium rate numbers and has tried to raise public awareness about the scams. "We now want to ensure there are even stronger safeguards for our customers, who we would urge to make use of these new options to protect themselves," said Gavin Patterson, group managing director for consumer the arm of BT. Both schemes have been undergoing trials in Ireland, and will be made available to 20 million BT customers from May.

Ring theatre promises 'perfect fit' for Sam Raimi's horror classics

But UK fans will just have to make do with a recording of the Broadway show

There will be no UK premiere for the musical adaptation of Sam Raimi's horror trilogy Rings. 

The stage version of his cult films about haunted video tapes premiered on Broadway in March, but its UK run was postponed due to the coronavirus pandemic. 

Producer David Kramer has now confirmed that the UK premiere will no longer go ahead. Instead, he plans to release a recorded version of the Broadway production.

"With the uncertainty of the theatre market, and the gaps in theatre scheduling, particularly at the Shaftesbury Theatre, we have made the decision not to bring Ring Circles to the UK," Kramer said in a statement.

"Over the last few months we have explored all possibilities to reschedule a UK run, but with the ongoing situation we have made the difficult decision that a filmed version of the Broadway show will be made available for UK audiences."

The show's description reads: "Five college students stumble upon a mysterious video and watch as zombies slaughter their friends. They then discover that the video retains its power after they've watched it, killing anyone who views it exactly seven days later.

"The group then has a week to figure out how to break the cycle and defeat the evil entity known as Samara."

The stage adaptation is by Rob Kemp, who also stars as Sam, and is directed by Randall Kramer.

The recorded version of the Broadway production will be available later this year.

African-American rapper   Curtis Jackson,  aka 50 Cent, has achieved the rare feat of occupying the  top five positions on Billboard's digital songs chart.

The  rapper, who has reigned the rap  charts  for the past decade, achieved the impressive  sweep  with  individual songs from his  recently released album "Animal Ambition."

The album,  released in April, was his fifth studio recording and the first in three years.

All  10 tracks  from the  album  made the chart, including the  title  track "Animal Ambition" at number six,  "Smoke" at  number seven and "9 shots" at   number 10.

Only one other artist has come close to achieving the top-five sweep.

In  2009, The Beatles occupied the top  six spots  on the  digital song chart following the release of their best-selling album "The Beatles: Rock  Band."

The 50-year-old rapper, who started his career in 2000, has sold more than 30 million  albums worldwide.

He has  also acted in films including 2005's "Get Rich  or Die Tryin'" and the television series "Power."

Liverpool manager Rafael Benitez has ordered captain Steven Gerrard not to play down Their Champions League ambitions and be more positive.
Gerrard told the BBC Liverpool were unlikely to win the trophy this year. Benitez responded: "I spoke to Steven and said to him that in future it's better to think we can win the Champions League. Why not?" He said: "We need winners here and everyone thinking only of Winning. I always want to win."
Benitez added: "When we lose I only think of solutions. If you only think about winning the next game, You don't know what the draw will be. "If we can win the next game, maybe we will draw a side that isn't so strong, or a side with injuries or suspensions." benitez is Hoping To win His first trophy since arriving at Liverpool from Valencia when they play Chelsea in the Carling Cup on Sunday in Cardiff.

The festive season has seen a surge in box office numbers, with the comedy sequel "Little Fockers" leading the pack. The film, which is the third installment in the "Meet the Parents" series, has topped the UK and Ireland box office chart over the Christmas period.

"Little Fockers", starring Ben Stiller, Robert De Niro and Owen Wilson, raked in £3.1m between Boxing Day and New Year's Eve, according to film industry analysts. The film's success is a testament to the enduring popularity of the franchise, which began with "Meet the Parents" in 2000 and continued with "Meet the Fockers" in 2004.

The film's plot revolves around the comedic trials and tribulations of Greg Focker (Stiller) as he navigates the challenges of parenthood and dealing with his overbearing father-in-law, played by De Niro. The film's light-hearted and family-friendly humour seems to have struck a chord with audiences looking for some festive cheer.

In second place on the chart was the latest installment of the "Narnia" series, "The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader", which took £1.8m. The film, based on the beloved children's books by C.S. Lewis, has been a consistent performer since its release in early December.

Rounding out the top three was Disney's "Tangled", a retelling of the Rapunzel fairy tale, which earned £1.3m. The animated film has been praised for its stunning visuals and engaging storytelling, appealing to both children and adults alike.

The festive period is traditionally a strong time for the film industry, with families flocking to cinemas for some holiday entertainment. This year's chart shows a diverse range of films succeeding, from comedy sequels to fantasy adventures and animated tales.

As we move into the new year, it remains to be seen whether "Little Fockers" can maintain its lead, or if a new contender will rise to the top of the box office chart. Regardless, it's clear that the Fockers have once again proven to be a hit with audiences, providing plenty of laughs and festive fun.

A landmark injunction has barred a firm from adding the phrase "Collins" in its name after a judicial challenge by the editors of the Collins Dictionary.

The High Court found that the use of the phrase by London-based firm Hutchinson & Company was "unlikely to cause controversy" with the dictionary brand.

The ruling is believed to be the last year a firm has been banned from employing a common Welsh phrase in its name.

Harper & Company had been using the abbreviation until 2006, but the publishers of the dictionary, Penguin, brought legal action in 2016.

The judge found that the use of the phrase "Collins" by the firm was "unlikely to cause controversy" with the dictionary brand and "took unfair advantage" of the goodwill associated with the name.

The ruling means that the firm will have to modify its name and logo.

HarperCollins stated it was "satisfied" with the ruling and that it will "aggressively protect" its trademarks.

Smith & Company stated it was "happy" with the ruling and was discussing its alternatives.

Banned American sprinter Kelli White says she knowingly took steroids given to her by Bay Area Lab Co-Operative (Balco) president Victor Conte. 

Conte faces a federal trial next year on charges of distributing steroids and tax evasion, and White said at first he tried to cover up what he was doing. 

"He's the one who told me that it wasn't what he said it was," White said in the San Francisco Chronicle. But she added: "It was my decision to go to him, not anybody else's." White said Conte at first told her the substance was flaxseed oil, only to change his story later. 

White failed a drugs test after winning the 100m and 200m titles at the 2003 world athletics championships. She was subsequently handed a two-year ban in May this year and has admitted taking the stimulant modafinil. At first, White claimed she took the drug to combat narcolepsy but she now takes full responsibility for her actions. 

"My whole belief about Victor is that he was selling a product," White said in the LA Times. "Whether it be a good product or a bad product, he was selling a product." White was introduced to Conte through her coach Remy Korchemy, who is also a defendant in the Balco case. 

The 27-year-old believes doping is so common in sport she felt compelled to cheat herself if she was to have any chance of winning. 

"I have no clue what it's going to take to change that," said White. 

"I would say I made a mistake and I would never, ever go back. "I would never recommend anyone to take that route."

Video phones are becomeing increasingly popular as a tool for people to meet potential partners, a new study has found.

The research, carried out by the University of Essex, found that people are using the technology to connect with others in a way that is similer to speed dating.

Lead researcher Dr Tom Buchanan said the findings showed that video phones could be used to help people find love.

"It's clear that people are using video phones to meet potential partners in a way that is similer to speed dating," he said.

"The technology provides a new way for people to connect with others and this could be used to help people find love."

The study, which is published in the journal New Media & Society, found that people who used video phones to meet potential partners were more likely to be open to the idea of a relationship and were more likely to exchange personal information.

Dr Buchanan said the findings suggested that video phones could be used to help people find love.

"The technology provides a new way for people to connect with others and this could be used to help people find love," he said.

The Gambling Commission is introducing new rules to ban "unfair practices" by casinos in the UK.

The regulator said it was concerned about "enticements" which persuaded people to continue gambling, such as free drinks and VIP treatment.

It is also clamping down on "loot boxes" in video games, which it said could lead to children gambling.

The new rules will come into force in October.

Gambling Commission chairman, Bill Moyes, said: "Our new approach will ensure gambling businesses stop using unfair techniques to exploit any errors customers make when gambling, and help make gambling fairer and safer."

He added that the regulator would be " taking action" if any company was found to be in breach of the new rules.

The Gambling Commission said it would be working with the government to make sure the rules were enforced.

'Loot boxes'

The regulator has also published new guidance on "loot boxes" - virtual containers which can be bought by gamers and which give them the chance to win prizes.

It said it was concerned that children might be tempted to gamble if they did not know what was in the boxes.

" loot boxes" have already been banned in Belgium and the Netherlands.

A number of video game companies, including EA, publisher of Fifa and Battlefield, have introduced "loot boxes" in their games.

In a statement, EA said it was "committed to providing players with a fair, fun and secure experience".

It added that all of its games "thoroughly tested and comply with applicable laws".

Ρublіс trust іn tахеs іs brеаkіng dоwn bесаusе Lаbоur аnd Тоrіеs аrе nоt bеіng strаіght wіth реорlе оn thе іssuе, Lіb Dеm lеаdеr Сhаrlеs Κеnnеdу hаs sаіd.
Α dау аhеаd оf thе gоvеrnmеnt's рrе-Вudgеt rероrt, Μr Кеnnеdу usеd а sреесh tо sау hіs раrtу wаs fасіng uр tо "раіnful есоnоmіс rеаlіtіеs". Ηе sаіd thе сurrеnt lеvеl оf tахаtіоn wаs аbоut rіght, аlthоugh hе wоuld рut а nеw 50% tах оn tор еаrnеrs. Οthеr раrtіеs hаvе ассusеd thе Lіb Dеms оf mаkіng unсоstеd рrоmіsеs. Μr Κеnnеdу mаdе іt сlеаr hе wаs dеtеrmіnеd tо соuntеr thаt ассusаtіоn. Τhе Lіb Dеms hаvе аlrеаdу рublіshеd whаt thеу sау аrе thе full соstіngs fоr аll thеіr рlаns аnd Wеdnеsdау's sреесh dіd nоt аnnоunсе nеw роlісіеs.
Sреаkіng аt thе Соmmоnwеаlth Сlub, Мr Κеnnеdу sаіd іt wаs сrіtісаl fоr а роlіtісаl раrtу tо hаvе есоnоmіс сrеdіbіlіtу, bоth оn whаt іt рrоmіsеd аnd whаt іt wаs ехресtеd tо dеlіvеr. Ηе sаіd. "Βudgеts hаvе tо аdd uр. Тоugh сhоісеs аrе nееdеd іn рublіс sреndіng." Тhе Lіb Dеms wоuld сut "lоw рrіоrіtу" sреndіng, іnсludіng thе gоvеrnmеnt's ІD саrds sсhеmе аnd thе Сhіld Τrust Fund.
Тhоsе сutbасks wоuld frее uр funds fоr іnсrеаsіng bаsіс stаtе реnsіоns fоr оvеr-75s, рuttіng mоrе роlісе оn thе strееts аnd rеіntrоduсіng fее еуе аnd dеntаl сhесks, hе sаіd. Τhе Lіb Dеms аrguе thеу wеrе hоnеst аbоut tахеs іn thе раst bу саllіng fоr а 1р rіsе оn іnсоmе tах. Νоw thеу sау thе оnlу sіmрlе tах rіsе thеу wаnt іs а nеw 50% tах bаnd fоr tор еаrnеrs tо рау fоr sсrарріng unіvеrsіtу tuіtіоn fееs, рrоvіdіng frее реrsоnаl саrе fоr еldеrlу аnd dіsаblеd реорlе аnd kееріng lосаl tахеs dоwn. Тhеrе wоuld аlsо bе а lосаl іnсоmе tах tо rерlасе соunсіl tах аnd а numbеr оf сhаngеs tо еnvіrоnmеntаl tахеs tо еnsurе іt іs thе "роllutеr whо рауs".
Τhе Lіb Dеms sау thе Τоrіеs hаvе оnlу lаіd оut роssіblе орtіоns fоr сuttіng tахеs tо grаb hеаdlіnеs whіlе Lаbоur hаs hіddеn mоst оf іts tах rіsеs. Μr Кеnnеdу sаіd: "Тhаt соntrасt wіth thе реорlе - thаt thе gоvеrnmеnt wіll оnlу tах fаіrlу аnd wіll sреnd thеіr mоnеу wіsеlу - саn оnlу bе sustаіnеd іf thе роlіtісаl раrtіеs аrе strаіghtfоrwаrd аbоut thеіr рlаns. "Wіth thе stеаlth tах strаtеgу оf Gоrdоn Βrоwn, thе оbvіоus unfаіrnеss оf оur сurrеnt tах sуstеm - еsресіаllу thе соunсіl tах, аnd thе еmрtу рrоmіsеs оf thе Соnsеrvаtіvе раrtу оn thіs іssuе - іt іs nо wоndеr thаt trust іn tахаtіоn іs brеаkіng dоwn." Ηе сhаllеngеd thе Τrеаsurу tо ореn uр іts bооks sо thе Νаtіоnаl Αudіt Οffісе саn rероrt оn thе gоvеrnmеnt's реrfоrmаnсе.
Соnsеrvаtіvе со-сhаіrmаn Lіаm Fох sаіd: Lіаm Fох sаіd "Іf Сhаrlеs Κеnnеdу іs sеrіоus аbоut mаkіng hіs budgеts аdd uр hе shоuld stаrt bу ехрlаіnіng hоw thеу wоuld fund thеіr 100 sреndіng соmmіtmеnts. "Тhе rеаlіtу іs, thе Lіb Dеms lасk thе соurаgе tо tасklе wаstе аnd burеаuсrасу, аnd thе оnlу реорlе whо wоuld fасе 'tоugh сhоісеs' wоuld bе thе fаmіlіеs whо wоuld bе £630 wоrsе оff а уеаr. " Αnd Сhаnсеllоr Gоrdоn Вrоwn sаіd thе Lіb Dеm fіgurеs dіd nоt аdd uр. Не ассusеd thе раrtу оf сlаіmіng іt wоuld sреnd lеss whіlе асrоss thе соuntrу соmmіttіng іtsеlf tо sреnd mоrе.

Manchester United have made an improved bid for Southampton's Luke Shaw, according to Sky sources. The Premier League champions are understood to be prepared to pay £30m plus add-ons for the england left back.

Sky Sports News HQ understands that Manchester United have offered £25m plus £5m in add-ons and that Southampton will not accept less than £30m.

southampton chairman Ralph Krueger has been told By Manager Ronald Koeman he must sell Shaw or risk losing him this summer.

United have already had two bids rejected for Shaw, who joined Saints from Southampton for £27m last year.

But They are now ready to make their third attempt at signing the 20-year-old defender after being rebuffed twice before.

In recent weeks, several high-profile MPs have publicly criticized the party's leadership and its handling of key issues.
